## Arctic Blast Snarls Thousands of Flights
Over 1[,000 flights were canceled](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJxskL2O6yAUhJ8Gd8eCE2ynoUhx3eUW2-x2FjGHmA0_ERCjffuVU285-jSj0fd5Xa7aeRfv_3UgVW1a9FrdTosAMQDKRXZGWSKkU0dKTHw44TQOU7cpa4WlUQi0xnI05xW5lOMw6RtJTcJ2TiFHyYUYBB_kMPZ41rdR39AQoZm4YJKTcTXl_ju5WDey_iftlPs1hc6rrdZnYacLw5nh3FrrQ4kHYzhThFdhONesd_IM50jtyEcdBOccXgWsd_etFlh1XMmTgTcN_gGN6EHRgHkR1ASFdsoEzcVKGUpNORxjOsPlIsJX_vfRZWWezXnvyjgxye9BO__-Wf-09hsAAP__OGtv8g) and 4,000 flights delayed over the weekend as subfreezing cold, winter storms, and strong winds battered much of the country, leaving millions under a winter weather advisory. An Arctic blast is dropping temperatures as much as 50 degrees below average across the U.S. Today's windchills could hit -17 in Des Moines, -10 in Kansas City, and 14 degrees in Dallas, according to [weather maps](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJxsj7GOtCAUhZ8GOgxcQcaC4m_s5m-3NFe5jmxQDLIy-_ab2Wm3PCcnOd_3cR_vGGLYH_9xI1eWNOJcwkWjEsoI0KPm3i1EQC0np6w0LdjOWL46NFb1nZ80eNODneUNJqNRgu49aVI8OJCgpVJGSaNN18ANpw4n8ETgrVRMS_KhpNx8prCXlZb4nS7KzZw2Ht1aynGy9h-DgcFQa22W9KyEZX1PXuU7iZ3qyWA4UsQsrpQLPQXmuYRZTBHPIr5O8YLh2fmjhhjD2Vmm5WPDEH_vyp_yPwEAAP__-aJbug). Along with the flight disruptions, the winter blast could impact voter turnout in Iowa, already caused a Sunday NFL game to be delayed until today, and is likely to [bring snow and ice](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJxskDGPozAQhX8NdBPZBkNSuLiGLtdeicZ4HPtkMLKHoM2vXyXFVtu84j3p6dP37z7fMaa4Pf7iSoZ9nnHh-KRZgtSg-rlvnfFEirqWjByF7tQ46LENxvsbOUTnJWHnB2vJ3gSNC-rRXQel22iUUL2QUkuhez1c1BXtgFY5IuVGIZtekIucy-V_jhsH8ukrP6lclry2yQTmvTbdn0ZNjZo4UIgpvbdGTSGvtNFZGzXh_gk4oMJPBVgWjgv4QvQiWPLGcTuoAmewCStDOB4E9UQOFbIHDgRHhTNygHpYeFHJwLTuVJCPQu_bthi3nzGlWIex6cVjxfgBavlXdd8BAAD___SEdBM) even to southern states.
## Congress Pushes Stopgap Deal to Avoid Shutdown
Congressional leaders have reached a deal to avert a government shutdown next week by agreeing on [a two-step stopgap funding bill](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJxs0LHO2yAUxfGngY0IrsEkA0MXb-na0bqGa0OFIQKSqG9fJfO3HekMP-n_577eMeVUjt94kht7XdGP9KJVCWUE6FXz4HYioImTU1aaCexsLI9OIyBNO8ANwxW2sM2azE1bHzyG4K88OZCgpVJGSaPNfIErbjNuEIggWKmYlhTSqO3yt6YyIu35X31Ru_h68uziGI_Opl8MFgbLiBRTzp-PwRLrSYXe_TufnRgsWkurtRS-lqNR76kWzCITBmpdNEIfRSDMYlSBL2pDHB-snFSG6PE5Qn0XBgtvLjzeKefUZ8u0PE5MX5aPHwP9DwAA___ZpWmx) that runs to early March. The government was set to begin shutting down on Friday before the agreement was made. It faces opposition from some Republicans who oppose stopgap funding. Speaker Mike Johnson, who backs the plan, was expected to [brief the GOP conference](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJxskLFuwyAUAL8Gb1jwDDgZGLqwpWtH69k821QYLCCx-vdVOnc_6U739ZgeGGJI2yceZNuaJ1xaeNEkudQc1KQ6b1cioKEjK0ehBxiNHrvdKukHlPN8M3Q3A-GscV21Mve7FiS96oIFAUpIqaXQSpsebjgbnMETgR-FZEqQDy2X_juH1HZa409-UemXfHTR7q2dlQ0fDBwDd11Xf-YYWljyG2DgEl2VgXtbGDghGTg5MHBLTluhWnkh9IEqj-g9FfL8wLLsfH0mH9LGT2zLzrHyuj-bz1fiMeejciHkoNVoumL9eYUYQzUjU2I7MMS_uPbvqt8AAAD__3JEaf8) last night. The bill would be the second round of temporary funding passed by Congress. The first one, shepherded by former Speaker Kevin McCarthy, helped [spark the GOP rebellion](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJxs0DFvwyAQBeBfg7ez4ADTDAxdvKVrRwuHs3MVMRaQWP33lTN3ecN7y6f3fZ2ugRNv61d4kG9LnsKt8YsmBcoCmsl00S9ESLojr5y0Gt1gXXf3FudgnJMmxosZ9GyWC2p30Vaj1Gh0xx4lGqmUVdIaO_T4EeYhzBiJMDqphJEUueXS_2Te2p2W9JtfVPpbfnTJ31vbq9CfAkeB43EcfaFno1LP_WxySVHg-KzvgFve1kK1ct5CgkQhUqkQ1kIEteV9DTsszy3ytsLMKUGhPZdW4WSCVKC0wLErPu4Hp8R1cMLI9RE4vUXt33_-AgAA__-y42aD) that led to McCarthy's ouster.
## Iowans Caucus Today to Kick Off 2024 Election
The first actual voting of the 2024 presidential election comes in [today's Iowa caucuses](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJxskLFuwyAQQL8Gb1jH2ZgsDF28pWtH6wznQIshsklQ_r5K585Penp6X9flSjHFfPuknW3dykKuxicvSiotcVzGztuNGXno2CoDekAzadMFq1aHozIInpQ2cCEGXN3F0QYG3LB20SLgCEppBXrUU48XWida0TOjN6DECOxjLUf_XWKugbf0Kk8-elf2LtlQ6_0Uw4fAWeDcWutdXt0bCpzfaoEzKIGzGgTONbCMpZF09HCPk09JB8uS5V6yp5cMfPApW6AqX-UhM7OXtcifXFof6p66w_p7iynFczJihNtOMf2V1H-__AYAAP__YzVjrg), as citizens gather to be counted at their local precincts in the face of sub-zero wind chills. The latest poll by the Des Moines Register shows 48% of likely GOP caucus-goers [selecting Donald Trump](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJxs0DFzhCAUBOBfgx0MPEFMQZHG7tKmdBCe50sQHMVz8u8zpk653zY7-_kYH54S5eeHX9HVuYw-VHrhqLgyHPSom-hmRMC2QaesNC3YzthmcX14s63qJrA9RKvCLI2Xpp1k20_B2tiQAwlaKmWUNNp0Ano_dX6CiAjRSsW0xEi17OKrUK4LzumnvHAXoaxNckut28HadwYDg-G6LkE54oY5Yq4iFHF-MxgyXsddlz1FBoNfcafgbzoPvpVElcKdYsk-RV73c91uT5zK5XnwZzgPPoG2vdZaLHVNze7idlFKdHSWaflcPaW_UfXfi34DAAD__4aUaJo) as their first choice. Nikki Haley now sits in 2nd place at 20%, an increase of 4 points since December. Ron DeSantis is in third at 16%. Vivek Ramaswamy is at 8%, but he has been crisscrossing the state, [visiting all 99 counties twice](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJxs0DGPrCAUBeBfg901gCi-wmIau3nNy8uWBuQ6soswgeuQ-fcbt97qFCc5-XI-7svd-ODj4685cKItLWYl_8JFgOhBqkU1btoQJXYNTkLzvpN66HWzT1o5yQ3vuR07NWprN2fHAbHTQhn1RzR-klwqLkQveK_6oZWjsYOx0iFKp7lgiqPzlHL7mXykHbfwTi_M7ZqOJkw70bOw7sbkzORca22PEq-OyRkjnIXJOWK9ItGOmcn5SNGZNxTwqRpYzbmeBQtUHwJYhIzP0wa_mlhg87kQrCkSFoK0Ae0IFxgw4Eo-RdgxIxSouyGgBF8xVSZnk-F2E8f_9z_T5Mk9r3FfBs0UfxzGhx89_frldwAAAP__z513XQ) hoping for a high-enough finish to propel him to New Hampshire, the next primary.

clean_body: Over 1 and 4,000 flights delayed over the weekend as subfreezing cold, winter storms, and strong winds battered much of the country, leaving millions under a winter weather advisory. | | | | | | MONDAY, JANUARY 15, 2024 ---|--- Good Morning! On this date in 2009, U.S. Airways pilot Chesley Sullenberger pulled off "The Miracle on the Hudson," safely putting his plane down in the Hudson River after a bird strike took out both engines. () It was a frigid weekend, and temps are about to drop down into "why do I live in this state, again? " territory--at least where The Editor resides. Do we have any Flyover readers who live in a tropical paradise and are trying not to laugh as you read about our frozen escapades? Drop us a line if you think your temperatures were the highest in all of Flyover Country this weekend (or the coldest!) Have you signed up for The Flyover yet? If not, you can fix that . Today's edition is brought to you by . | Arctic Blast Snarls Thousands of Flights Over 1 and 4,000 flights delayed over the weekend as subfreezing cold, winter storms, and strong winds battered much of the country, leaving millions under a winter weather advisory. An Arctic blast is dropping temperatures as much as 50 degrees below average across the U.S. Today's windchills could hit -17 in Des Moines, -10 in Kansas City, and 14 degrees in Dallas, according to . Along with the flight disruptions, the winter blast could impact voter turnout in Iowa, already caused a Sunday NFL game to be delayed until today, and is likely to even to southern states. Congress Pushes Stopgap Deal to Avoid Shutdown Congressional leaders have reached a deal to avert a government shutdown next week by agreeing on that runs to early March. The government was set to begin shutting down on Friday before the agreement was made. It faces opposition from some Republicans who oppose stopgap funding. Speaker Mike Johnson, who backs the plan, was expected to last night. The bill would be the second round of temporary funding passed by Congress. The first one, shepherded by former Speaker Kevin McCarthy, helped that led to McCarthy's ouster. Iowans Caucus Today to Kick Off 2024 Election The first actual voting of the 2024 presidential election comes in , as citizens gather to be counted at their local precincts in the face of sub-zero wind chills. The latest poll by the Des Moines Register shows 48% of likely GOP caucus-goers as their first choice. Nikki Haley now sits in 2nd place at 20%, an increase of 4 points since December. Ron DeSantis is in third at 16%. Vivek Ramaswamy is at 8%, but he has been crisscrossing the state, hoping for a high-enough finish to propel him to New Hampshire, the next primary. Enjoy reading The Flyover? mailto:?cc=editor@jointheflyover.com&subject=My%20new%20favorite%20newsletter!&body=Hey!%0D%0A%0D%0AI%E2%80%99ve%20been%20reading%20a%20new%20newsletter%20every%20morning%20called%20The%20Flyover.%20I%20love%20it!%20I%20think%20you%27ll%20love%20it%20too.%0D%0A%0D%0AIt%E2%80%99s%20my%20new%20favorite%20source%20for%20news%20first%20thing%20in%20the%20morning.%20They%20cover%20important%20and%20interesting%20stories%E2%80%94usually%20politics%2C%20sports%2C%20finance%2C%20science%20%26%20tech%2C%20etc.%0D%0A%0D%0AI%20think%20you%20should%20give%20it%20a%20try.%0D%0A%0D%0AJust%20copy%20this%20link%20and%20paste%20it%20into%20your%20browser%3A%20https%3A%2F%2Fjointheflyover.com%2F%3Futm%3D83%0D%0A%0D%0ALet%20me%20know%20if%20you%20like%20it.%20I%20think%20you%20will!%0D%0A to share with your friends and family. | ➤**Hunter Biden 's lawyer says his client is now willing to testify in a deposition-style hearing if the House issues a new subpoena. The offer comes after two House committees recommended Biden be cited for contempt. () ➤ President Biden 's approval rating dropped to 33%, a record low for any president in the last 15 years, in a new ABC News/Ipsos poll. Donald Trump's approval rating sits at 41%. () ➤ The House GOP says it will push for punishment of any federal worker who walks off the job to join a pro-Palestinian protest tomorrow. Staffers at nearly two dozen agencies have said they plan to walk out. () ➤ A federal judge ruled that a U.S. law barring people from carrying guns into post offices was unconstitutional. The ruling dismissed part of an indictment against a postal worker who brought a gun to work. () | Flying together with: Every day, data brokers profit from your sensitive info--phone number, DOB, SSN--. And who's buying it? Best case: companies target you with ads. Worst case: scammers and identity thieves. It's time you check out Incogni. It scrubs your personal data from the web, confronting the world's data brokers on your behalf. And unlike other services, Incogni helps from all broker types, including those tricky People Search Sites. Help protect yourself from identity theft, spam calls, ID theft and health insurers raising your rates. Plus, just for Flyover readers: . | ➤ In the NFC playoffs, the Packers had an easy 48-32 victory over the Cowboys while the Lions just edged past the Rams, 24-23. Here's a look at next weekend's brackets so far. () ➤ In the AFC, the Texans romped over the Browns 45-14, and the Chiefs defeated the Dolphins 26-7 in the NFL's 4th-coldest game. Texans' rookie quarterback C.J. Stroud became the youngest QB ever to win a playoff game. () ➤The Chiefs game was so cold that quarterback Patrick Mahomes' helmet cracked open after a tackle, and head coach Andy Reid's mustache was dripping with icicles. () ➤ Washington Huskies coach Kalen DeBoer will replace Nick Saban as head coach of Alabama's Crimson Tide. DeBoer took Washington to the college championship game this year but lost to Michigan. Four of the cars showed off the feature in choreographed spins in Las Vegas. () | ➤ **A Vermont state trooper plunged into icy waters to save a drowning 8-year-old in a feat captured on her body-cam. The child survived after several days recovering in the hospital. () ➤ A new photo project captures pictures of kids' school lunches from around the world, including from U.S. school cafeterias. Take a look and see which lunch you'd prefer. | | Friend, I hope you enjoy a meaningful Martin Luther King Jr. Day.
You may not know this, but it’s the only federal holiday designated as a National Day of Service to encourage all Americans to volunteer in support of their communities.
Volunteerism has a rich tradition in our country, but whether or not you’ll be supporting or participating in a service project, I hope you will join me in appreciating how far our country has come to secure equality under the law for people of all backgrounds.
Here’s to an American future of strength, freedom, and justice.
Regards,
Brad Finstad
_**“I have a dream that one day this nation will rise up and live out the true meaning of its creed: ‘We hold these truths to be self-evident: that all men are created equal.’”**_
**-Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.**

subject: Can you believe this?

body:
Tonight, Ron DeSantis will be the talk of the town at the FINAL town hall before the Iowa Caucus! All eyes will be on Fox News at 6 pm ET!
---
| | | | | | | | | | [ ](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb62a52d4b9c8f543441982626cd629a5ec83e030521a7d64441d64828bd23fa4f32f7e90134c2ad00d7d70fdfd3cb789be)
---
Hey Friend -- The Iowa Caucus is here! TONIGHT, the people of Iowa will be headed out to caucus for Ron DeSantis... it's time to get ready for the biggest night we've faced yet. Right now is the moment we've been fighting for. This is what it has all led up to. We cannot let up on the most critical day of our campaign yet. We have no time to waste to show our team in Iowa that they're not fighting alone. **___[_Before the evening kicks off, will you rush in $5, $10, $25, or anything you can to go into these caucuses with the momentum we need?_](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb62a52d4b9c8f543441982626cd629a5ec83e030521a7d64441d64828bd23fa4f32f7e90134c2ad00d7d70fdfd3cb789be)___** The DeSantis team in Iowa is _counting on us_ to back them up tonight NATIONWIDE. We cannot let them fight alone.
---
| | | | | [Rush in $10](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb679e75b52055c5268f2b12f4cce29ca55b2ec2cb3007b7d6b928883585aa1220702babf06219d9ff54d2ddc6ef83092d8)
---
| | | [Rush in $25](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb690cd6301f07d369975cf336cc698cb2dfa0c83710f95bcdc20bffa7b6d2dd4c6f62111f9a804b5e5b11a7a7b1a6f2ab2)
---
| | | | | [Rush in $50](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb65496533569dfde7e483a8bf4b66014a4d728bb29cd46de6b0072274563143a83113fb4f1d4528a32f48de95aeb81ed93)
---
| | | [Rush in $100](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb69ae7d4f238820e7ee45894f28c5fa77b491d4a2cfdf5467e5fe460e9677cf76dc8957baea62ec37fdc5132105c8d9158)
---
_This is our final chance to show the country that we are ready to prove every doubter of this campaign wrong. Iowans for DeSantis need to see us standing up alongside them_** _right now before they head out to caucus!_** **_**_**_[**_Rush in your support HERE before 7:00 PM!_**](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb62a52d4b9c8f543441982626cd629a5ec83e030521a7d64441d64828bd23fa4f32f7e90134c2ad00d7d70fdfd3cb789be)_**_**_** In Freedom,
Team DeSantis

---
| | | | [ ](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb62a52d4b9c8f543441982626cd629a5ec83e030521a7d64441d64828bd23fa4f32f7e90134c2ad00d7d70fdfd3cb789be)
---
| |
**🚨 ATTENTION 🚨** Our grassroots team drives our every success, and we know we ask a lot of you to help us win this critical election. As this race continues to heat up, we will be relying on our supporters to help us go the distance, and we want to make sure we respect your preferences when we reach out to you. If you'd like to receive fewer emails from our team, please click **[here.](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb684e4f956b07c905d7a03ba1fc16f752f909ba216a2cf6cc6dda1fed8fbd446e8cb5ae627e73b8caebecd0ad5142eef8c "here.")**

---

**Volunteer!**
We welcome you to join Team DeSantis by volunteering to spread our message: Decline is a choice. Success is attainable. And freedom is worth fighting for.

Help us lead our Great American Comeback by getting involved today!
---
| | [Sign up here!](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb657b5805b943002e3d3102f46b1d1baa170fd4f3a53e897ecb78bed429225f7c7bcc87ca43ff93345f843f341740baac1)
---
| | |
_**Get to Know Ron DeSantis**_
---
| | | [ ](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb62a52d4b9c8f543441982626cd629a5ec83e030521a7d64441d64828bd23fa4f32f7e90134c2ad00d7d70fdfd3cb789be)
---

Ron DeSantis comes from blue-collar roots in the state of Florida. Nothing was handed to him, he worked his way through college and led his team as the Yale Baseball captain. After earning his undergraduate degree, Ron DeSantis graduated from Harvard Law School, but was so moved by the events of September 11, 2001, that he volunteered to serve in the Navy to fight for our freedom. When he returned, he started a life of public service by serving Florida as both Congressman and now Governor. Despite all his years of fighting to ensure the American people enjoy the freedom we're promised in this country, he's watched the Left strip away freedoms at every turn and impose radical agendas on children, families, and consumers nationwide. In 2009, Ron met the love of his life Casey in Florida, and he's now the proud father of 6, 5, and 3-year-old children. Because of his kids, Ron DeSantis knows that this fight is _personal_ and understands the frustration parents face when teachers' unions and liberal groups shove a political agenda down the throats of children.

---
| [](https://click.email.rondesantis.com/?qs=77b8278358ef1bb62a52d4b9c8f543441982626cd629a5ec83e030521a7d64441d64828bd23fa4f32f7e90134c2ad00d7d70fdfd3cb789be)
---

On Day One, Ron DeSantis has _promised_ that he will bring a RECKONING for what unelected bureaucrats like Anthony Fauci and DC elites have done to children. Ron DeSantis will ignite our Great American Comeback by _immediately getting to work_ stopping the invasion across our southern border, ensuring our military once again puts the mission first, declaring our economic independence, and restoring our American energy dominance so that our country gets back on track before it's too late. In Florida, Ron DeSantis' record speaks for itself. He's taken on the political establishment, politicized bureaucratic agencies, woke corporations, and the legacy media countless times and **_won every single time_**. Under his leadership, Florida has cultivated a thriving economy. It has stood as a citadel of freedom, and with him as our next President, America can once again be that shining City on a Hill for the rest of the world to look upon. _That's why he's running for President of the United States. Ron DeSantis knows that the decline of our country is a choice that cannot continue for another four years; the time to lead is now.... the time to act is now._

---
|
---

Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and name of employer for each individual whose aggregate contributions exceed $200 in an election cycle. The maximum that an individual may contribute is $6,600 ($3,300/primary & $3,300/general). The maximum that a couple using a joint account may contribute is $13,200 ($6,600/primary & $6,600/general). A federal multi-candidate PAC may contribute $10,000 ($5,000/primary & $5,000/general). Contribution rules
* I am a U.S. citizen or lawfully admitted permanent resident (i.e., green card holder).
* This contribution is made from my own funds, and funds are not being provided to me by another person or entity for the purpose of making this contribution.
* I am making this contribution with my own personal credit card and not with a corporate or business credit card or a card issued to another person.
* I am at least eighteen years old. I am not, nor am I making this contribution on behalf of, a corporation, labor organization, national bank, foreign national without a green card, a federal contractor, or any other federally impermissible source.

name: Freedom Nation Today
subject: Learn about the real American Founding
body:
This course goes about this task by examining the actual words and actions of the Founders

| | | This sponsored message from Freedom Nation Today is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=5bda3a2a15a74d26a8418bed3ec98268&_e=X14VPO1Ii4YHG_jf9rApSL3jMQK2CzfMG2Icj_vm5k2dM2FKjzcIP8UqMJQkVyqzpOij0hJvnSv0itAosMTNytqW1F1vNBUV949zsOTbX6OzJAt4nnZ3TIS9cil_smZDP7DZjIRqrL10yc51APGNCeXgXPyjouVwHbpbpp6phy4E1JIC2HVF9U5zHy5G1SpooZcD_d6M8NWk8Pyj4YZ4UMX5V0XyWfTIUEl_yGI4aS_xXjEriQwKdNkE-4_nBrPyVIgnvUYOnwdGhjkPI_JLulQxEnnw43cOVYYnju5AtPFxxWglk9e_QyFc1d2EUdPKyl2AB0WCJ_VmPHrvfkX_rqxTKcYruHJBdYABXJfYSEkwKXhM8oAgcztTH1c7lckPE6GDXsXc2M-kSGSf3V3UctC0Zav-HLAoas2pbdCgGlYFqWWD_Q0B_YucEowdj4Lg)
---
| |
Hello Friend,

Thomas G. West, professor of politics at Hillsdale College, is one of the great scholars and teachers of the American Founding.

That's why we're so excited to offer a course that explains his core teachings about the American Founding, **which is now available on DVD.**

In this course, Dr. West sits down with David Azerrad, his former student and current professor at Hillsdale's Graduate School of Government, to reveal the truth about the American Founding.

As Dr. West explains, the Founders' political theory is so foreign to Americans today--even to scholars and teachers of American history and politics--that the task of uncovering the truth about the Founding is like unearthing something from a "deep and long-forgotten pit."

This course goes about this task by examining the actual words and actions of the Founders, and it brings their theory to life by comparing it to the dominant views of contemporary America.

The result is an exciting and often surprising account of how the Founders established a free government in America and what they thought was necessary to sustain it.

As you know, our online courses are free. That's why we depend on the generosity of people like you to help us share the course with as many people as possible.

Would you consider making a tax-deductible donation to help promote this course?

And now, for any donation of $100 or more, you will receive this beautiful DVD box set for **" The Real American Founding: A Conversation,"** by clicking the link below.

[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/real-american-founding-dvd](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=5bda3a2a15a74d26a8418bed3ec98268&_e=X14VPO1Ii4YHG_jf9rApSL3jMQK2CzfMG2Icj_vm5k2dM2FKjzcIP8UqMJQkVyqzpOij0hJvnSv0itAosMTNytqW1F1vNBUV949zsOTbX6OzJAt4nnZ3TIS9cil_smZDP7DZjIRqrL10yc51APGNCeXgXPyjouVwHbpbpp6phy4E1JIC2HVF9U5zHy5G1SpooZcD_d6M8NWk8Pyj4YZ4UMX5V0XyWfTIUEl_yGI4aS_xXjEriQwKdNkE-4_nBrPyVIgnvUYOnwdGhjkPI_JLuhDWB73LfbtAI0VfqjINPClx7efiyxNOwF4EaN8DE5DqbgtFue5ANGwhyGxY_VkFDxaVRoJSCLvmmT-mPnKLZ-kQZeqN5fZC1MyPIzNNn1dfhr-KGuA4MGwFWCee3-c04YvsxP1l2JZh0J_SmoiuIWKn4xi5bPZRCHjtSidKJoTg)

I hope you'll join us in this fun and important work of sharing the truth about the American Founding with your fellow Americans.

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, '09
Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College

---

| |

subject: Large-Scale Attacks On Houthi Targets Still Reflect Biden Admin Priority To Avoid Escalation, Experts Say - American Update
body:
And Emperor Biden’s Latest The State Goes Marching In To Seize Patents

https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c680397%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=BpiPHRNcF8enGYxIXzguXA&sysid=1 Large-Scale Attacks On Houthi Targets Still Reflect Biden Admin Priority To Avoid Escalation, Experts Say - American Update

Long-awaited retaliatory strikes on Houthi-linked targets in Yemen, though expansive, underscore the Biden administration’s characteristic reticence to avoid provoking Iran, experts told the Daily Caller News Foundation.

If it ain’t broke, break it. This is Joe Biden’s guiding principle. He took President Donald J. Trump’s much-tighter southern border and ripped it as wide open as a gutted trout’s belly. Biden turned Trump’s energy independence into begging Iran and Venezuela to pump more oil.

U.S. fighter aircraft took down an anti-ship cruise missile the Houthi rebels in Yemen fired toward a U.S. Navy destroyer on Sunday, the military said. The interception took place near the coast of al-Hudaydah, Yemen’s main port city, U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) said in a statement.

name: Hillsdale College via American Action News
subject: Our course on the American Founding—Now on DVD
body:
I'd like to invite you to join me in Hillsdale College's newest free course studying the greatest Christian apologist of the 20th century, C.S. Lewis.

| | |
---
| | | | This sponsored message from American Action News is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zl4u20llugv2jfc04a97rg6t1ccsqoj6ig84crc0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpj61o30c3h2bs) [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zd3qho1cc8ndgaetbn87f92gkgop1aen9pivtko0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpj61o30c3h2bs) | Hello Friend,

Thomas G. West, professor of politics at Hillsdale College, is one of the great scholars and teachers of the American Founding.

That's why we're so excited to offer a course that explains his core teachings about the American Founding, **which is now available on DVD.**

In this course, Dr. West sits down with David Azerrad, his former student and current professor at Hillsdale's Graduate School of Government, to reveal the truth about the American Founding. | **
### What students are saying
** “I appreciate the brevity of explanation and new information in a way I hadn't known or realized myself. The quizzes are helpful. Having two teachers converse brings an interesting perspective.” **- Darlena** “It helps put into context the thinking of our founders at that time. That’s very important and misunderstood by so many people today!” **- Robert**
---|---

As Dr. West explains, the Founders' political theory is so foreign to Americans today--even to scholars and teachers of American history and politics--that the task of uncovering the truth about the Founding is like unearthing something from a "deep and long-forgotten pit."

This course goes about this task by examining the actual words and actions of the Founders, and it brings their theory to life by comparing it to the dominant views of contemporary America.

The result is an exciting and often surprising account of how the Founders established a free government in America and what they thought was necessary to sustain it.

As you know, our online courses are free. That's why we depend on the generosity of people like you to help us share the course with as many people as possible.

Would you consider making a tax-deductible donation to help promote this course?

And now, for any donation of $100 or more, you will receive this beautiful DVD box set for **" The Real American Founding: A Conversation,"** by clicking the link below.

[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/real-american-founding-dvd](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zpv4582obs25qcpb57sa041vpntiekebiuvlmfo8_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpj61o30c3h2bs)

I hope you'll join us in this fun and important work of sharing the truth about the American Founding with your fellow Americans.

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, '09
Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

---

name: John James
subject: THIS IS A NEW LOW
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John James for Michigan
body:
I’m disappointed but FAR from surprised.

| | | [](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zuqns8rpnks5dfnst2u1os476r3n242rg5hl0c0g_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgm0pr2c5h2bs)
---
Democrats stooped to a new low... Friend, did you see that one of the corrupt liberal career politicians running for my seat DARKENED MY SKIN COLOR in a request for campaign donations? Democrats are coming at me in droves—and one of them, an indicted former judge (where do they find these characters?!), darkened my skin color in a pathetic attempt to fund his campaign. I’m disappointed but FAR from surprised. But what can you expect from another one of these hypocritical white liberals who is literally trying to defeat the only Black congressman from the State of Michigan? He just can’t stand the fact that I’m a Republican! Democrats are so scared that Black people are beginning to wake up and realize that Democrats are the party of prejudice—so they’re resorting to dirty games! That’s why I need you to stand by me.
---
[**STAND BY ME**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zrjt9bm4tq8lama6afaq6epocvb67ace6f81f6fg_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgm0pr2c5h2bs)
---
I didn’t put my life on the line flying combat missions as an Army Ranger-qualified Apache pilot in Iraq to have the Radical Left try to define me by my skin color alone. He’s using an old racist trope by darkening my skin to get votes... this coming from the party that likes to call Republicans racist. HA! Hypocrites. From MIT to Macomb County, liberal elites like my opponent believe the same thing: whether you’re Black or whether you’re conservative—or BOTH, like me—they’re better than us, they’re smarter than us, and we should just shut up and fall in line. Well that’s a load of BS! He’s focused on using racial tropes to win his primary. I’m focused on serving the people. That’s why, I need your help to support my conservative campaign. Will you join me?
---
[**JOIN ME**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z9t8c528794cmpjmvhesppctllln7hc0isafr190_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgm0pr2c5h2bs)
---
Thank you, John James
---
| | Paid for by John James for Michigan
---

Friend,

**We made it! Today is the day Iowans have the privilege of caucusing for their preferred candidate to be the 47th President of the United States.**

Iowa's First in the Nation status is one we don't take lightly. It is so important for everyone to participate regardless of their candidate of choice. Your vote signifies who you think is best to beat Joe Biden in November.

subject: Re: Notes from the Campaign Trail

body:
Glimpses of each week on the road to the White House

|
---
| | |
---
[](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=notes_1_15_24_email_4rs_1_nd&n=1)
|
---
* * *
# Notes from the Campaign Trail
Edition #4 from Digital Messaging Director, Jonathan Hiller
* * *
* * *
Peter —
I never thought I’d re-enter presidential politics. I was a filmmaker with Hillary Clinton’s campaign in 2016 and followed her around the country for a year, creating short documentaries and digital ads. As the campaign progressed and I observed the egregious way in which the DNC treated Bernie Sanders, I couldn’t stomach it anymore. I resigned in disgust. I could no longer be part of the Machine—the democracy-devouring duopoly. I saw Trump’s election as a perfectly reasonable response of an American electorate completely fed up with a gridlocked, corrupt system. Trump promised to break things—and people wanted them broken. I did not support Trump, but I spent the following years seeking to understand the people who voted for him.
I produced a documentary about water rights, but at its core, the film was about the plight of the small town. In our reckless pursuit of globalism, we’ve seemed to have forgotten our ties to our communities and the heartfelt people in them. I was at an impasse, politically. I waded into the paradox of agreeing with some policies on both sides and was excoriated by my groupthink-oriented friends. No political party is a totalizing discourse, I knew, but I felt alone. So, I registered as an Independent and, for the most part, kept my mouth shut.
When my friend Charles Eisenstein joined Bobby’s campaign, I started paying attention to national politics again. How could Charles, with his brilliant mind and spirit, want to participate in a broken political system? I reached out.
“What is going on here? Who is Bobby Kennedy?”
“Look into him...”
So, I did. I went down a rabbit hole. What I found was fascinating: an environmental lawyer and avid civil rights advocate; a man in recovery from addiction (I’m 17 years sober myself); a person who speaks openly about his relationship with the divine. And, most importantly, a leader offering a cogent audit of the ails of our fragile democracy—more so than anyone I’ve ever heard from running for president. I was filled with a sense of hope that had been dormant for years.
“How can I help?” I said to Charles.
I now find myself as Director of Digital Messaging for Bobby’s campaign, guiding our video production, email communications, social media, and texting program. I feel humbled and fortunate to serve every day. Collaborating with integrity also includes healthy conflict, and we don’t all agree on everything. I find it refreshing that Bobby himself encourages this kind of dialogue, and I feel privileged to work for a political candidate with whom I disagree on some issues, but feel the emotional permission to voice my concerns when called. I cannot stress enough how _different_ this feels. >From Bobby’s heartfelt expressions of _I’m grateful for you_ to this team’s willingness to jump into any problem with joy and resolve, we are a family putting in one thousand percent every day to help elect a person we know will bring an end to the corruption—the sort that prevents the people of this country from getting what they need to not only survive, but to thrive.
What that looks like to me is a reflection of the entrepreneurial spirit this country was founded upon. We are radical innovators. We are rebels. We think and act independently of the status quo. This originative spirit should be rewarded by a system built to encourage this kind of dialogue, not weigh it down with red tape. And for those of us who want to work to live, rather than live to work, we should be able to thrive working one job with honesty and integrity.
A humble request: If this story stirs in you the idea of hope, [please consider clicking a few buttons and sending $5 our way](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=notes_1_15_24_email_4rs_1_nd&n=2). We are eternally grateful for your support; because, as our leader says, _this is about you_. We are all working tirelessly on your behalf—on this country’s behalf—to help deliver you a president who will not break things, but fix them.
| |
---
---
Warmly,
Jonathan Hiller
Team Kennedy
[TeamKennedy.com](https://www.kennedy24.com/?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=notes_1_15_24_email_4rs_1_nd&n=3)

Peter,
Today is a good day because today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day.

Today I am reminded of the powerful words and actions of a man who dedicated his life to the pursuit of justice, equality, and unity. Dr. King's legacy serves as a beacon of inspiration, guiding us to strive for a more just and inclusive society.

On this special day, let us take a moment to reflect on the progress we have made as a nation and the work that still lies ahead.

Together, we can build bridges of communication and work towards a future where everyone is treated with dignity and equality.

As your representative in Congress, I am dedicated to advancing policies that promote justice, equality, and opportunity for all Americans. I believe that by working together, we can overcome challenges and create a brighter future for the generations to come.

Sincerely,

Roger Williams is a Christian, conservative, pro-life, and second amendment supporter taking Texas values to Washington, D.C. He is a small business owner with over 45 years of experience and is running for Congress to create economic growth, jobs, and provide strong leadership. Roger worked alongside President Trump to fight against high taxes, rising energy prices, and soaring healthcare costs. His passion for conservative leadership led him to support Republican candidates from the White House to the county courthouse. Roger serves on the Board of Trustees for the Bush School of Government and Public Service at Texas A&M University, the Texas Christian University Board of Trustees, and the National Football Foundation and College Football Hall of Fame Board of Directors.

clean_body: | | | | Peter, Today is a good day because today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day. Today I am reminded of the powerful words and actions of a man who dedicated his life to the pursuit of justice, equality, and unity. Dr. King's legacy serves as a beacon of inspiration, guiding us to strive for a more just and inclusive society. On this special day, let us take a moment to reflect on the progress we have made as a nation and the work that still lies ahead. Together, we can build bridges of communication and work towards a future where everyone is treated with dignity and equality. As your representative in Congress, I am dedicated to advancing policies that promote justice, equality, and opportunity for all Americans. I believe that by working together, we can overcome challenges and create a brighter future for the generations to come. Sincerely, Rep. Roger Williams TX-25 | Roger Williams is a Christian, conservative, pro-life, and second amendment supporter taking Texas values to Washington, D.C. He is a small business owner with over 45 years of experience and is running for Congress to create economic growth, jobs, and provide strong leadership. Roger worked alongside President Trump to fight against high taxes, rising energy prices, and soaring healthcare costs. His passion for conservative leadership led him to support Republican candidates from the White House to the county courthouse. Roger serves on the Board of Trustees for the Bush School of Government and Public Service at Texas A&M University, the Texas Christian University Board of Trustees, and the National Football Foundation and College Football Hall of Fame Board of Directors. | "Facebook" | "Twitter" ---|--- | | ⬇DONATE BY CHECK⬇ ROGER WILLIAMS FOR U.S. CONGRESS 10 N. By joining Team Williams, you will make a difference in your community. | A Note For You, From Roger My commitment to our great state and country is unwavering, and I am grateful to have my wife Patty, our two daughters, and YOU standing beside me on this journey. | Williams For Congress 10 N. Caddo St, PMB #174, Cleburne, TX 76031 ==

name: Larry Klayman
subject: BREAKING: FBI arrests Babylon Bee actor, terrifies his pregnant wife
body:
Just a short time ago, four FBI agents arrested my friend and client, Siaka Massaquoi, at the airport.

| [](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zdn6tm0qgc9k7p1b8bf0i36rdf1pvmqo04mu4jso_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgm8or4c9h2bs)
---
| |
---
[](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zm7nsas1bbepm2g8gn6v7aoj1o8958akvh23gmf0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgm8or4c9h2bs)Dear Patriot,

**[Please give a crucial $25, $50, $100 or more right now to help Babylon Bee actor and patriot, Siaka Massaquoi, fight back against Joe Biden's FBI.](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zjtrap7psmeioa5jbejes2se4snamsibvclp25u8_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgm8or4c9h2bs)**

Just a short time ago, four FBI agents arrested my friend and client, Siaka Massaquoi, at the airport.

He was traveling home with his wife, Charlotte, and their unborn baby, after they attended an event hosted by the conservative news site, Daily Wire.

The FBI refused to show Charlotte the arrest warrant.

**Siaka was arrested because he 's a black patriot who can persuade many Democrat voters to vote against Joe Biden on Election Day. **

You see, not only is Siaka a gifted actor (he's starred in Babylon Bee's funny series, "Californians Adjust to New Life In Texas") ...

... He's also the Vice Chair of the Los Angeles County GOP and a peaceful patriot who briefly appeared at the peaceful protest the stolen presidential election at the U.S. Capitol on January 6, 2021.

Siaka is also bold as a lion about his faith in Jesus Christ, his love for his family, and his deep concern for our country.

Of course, all that makes him an enemy of Joe Biden's lawless administration.

**So the FBI arrested Siaka for spending 76 seconds inside the U.S. Capitol. And charged him with four misdemeanors! That 's right, MISDEMEANORS!**

What's more, this isn't the first time the FBI has stomped on Siaka's rights, either.

**Over twenty FBI agents raided Siaka 's home with drawn guns on June 10, 2021 at 5:45 AM, and handcuffed him, his best friend, and two other roommates. **

As you can imagine, his best friend's two little children, just three and seven years old, were _terrified._

And here's what happened next:

* The FBI agents refused to let Siaka speak to his lawyer
* The FBI agents refused to show him the arrest warrant
* The FBI agents grilled him and his friends for FIVE HOURS with no lawyers present!
* The FBI confiscated his cell phone, computer and his MAGA hat and has refused ever since to give them back!
* And we have reason to believe that Siaka and the other peaceful J-6 protesters were illegally and unconstitutionally surveilled!

**And because the FBI didn 't arrest or charge Siaka, he had no legal grounds to find out why they raided his home...terrorized him, his friends, his friend's little children... abused his Constitutionally protected rights...and stole his property.**

Can you imagine being abused like this... _as an American citizen in your own country?_

What the FBI did to my peaceful, innocent client in 2021 and just a short time ago is a CRIME.

**That 's why I've filed a crucial lawsuit that PROVES the FBI and other agencies carried out ILLEGAL SURVEILLANCE on January 6 patriots. That means their criminal convictions can be THROWN OUT. And their criminal trials MUST STOP.**

I'm Larry Klayman, founder and general counsel for Freedom Watch.

I'm a former federal prosecutor and trial lawyer who's fought for the American people for 46 years.

You've seen me on Fox News, and other cable news outlets.

I'm proud to say I won a court ruling that President Bill Clinton committed a crime when he illegally the White House files of Kathleen Willey. (I'm the only lawyer in American history to have a court rule that a sitting President had committed a crime)

I worked closely with Congressman Bob Barr and other impeachment managers during the impeachment of criminal Bill Clinton.

And I successfully represented the victims of other Clinton scandals, like Filegate, Travelgate, IRS Gate, Chinagate, and many others.

**I know what it takes to take on federal agencies, politicians, even presidents, and win.****And with this critically important case, I can PROVE IN COURT that Attorney General Merrick Garland, FBI Director Christopher Wray, their departments and agencies at the U.S. Department of Justice ordered and carried out ILLEGAL SURVEILLANCE OF LAW-ABIDING AMERICAN CITIZENS!**

Siaka wants to use this critically important lawsuit to speak up for other patriots, too, because he's alarmed at what Joe Biden and his friends are doing to our country.

He's deeply concerned that our country is going down a very dark road...and I agree.

I want to fix this horrifying situation peacefully and legally in the courts...before it's too late.

body:

This revealing video with Marine Corp Veteran, Brian Tally, delves into the controversy surrounding Dan Crenshaw's actions against fellow Navy SEAL Eddie Gallagher.
With increasing voices from the veteran community speaking out against Crenshaw, this video sheds light on a side of the story often untold.
Did Crenshaw, a decorated SEAL, prioritize politics over brotherhood?
Let's talk about the implications of Dan's decisions, raising crucial questions about leadership, loyalty, and integrity like...
"If Dan actively worked against a fellow brother in arms, how's he actively working against his constituents?"

name: Hillsdale College via Your America News
subject: I hope you’ll join us
body:
I'd like to invite you to join me in Hillsdale College's newest free course studying the greatest Christian apologist of the 20th century, C.S. Lewis.

| | |
---
| | | | This sponsored message from Your America News is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38155%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=4vJJGQuSxYxsJbsWS8ZL2g&sysid=1) [](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38155%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=4vJJGQuSxYxsJbsWS8ZL2g&sysid=1) | Hello Friend,

Thomas G. West, professor of politics at Hillsdale College, is one of the great scholars and teachers of the American Founding.

That’s why we’re so excited to offer a course that explains his core teachings about the American Founding, **which is now available on DVD.**

In this course, Dr. West sits down with David Azerrad, his former student and current professor at Hillsdale’s Graduate School of Government, to reveal the truth about the American Founding. | **
### What students are saying
** “I appreciate the brevity of explanation and new information in a way I hadn’t known or realized myself. The quizzes are helpful. Having two teachers converse brings an interesting perspective.” **- Darlena** “It helps put into context the thinking of our founders at that time. That’s very important and misunderstood by so many people today!” **- Robert**
---|---

As Dr. West explains, the Founders’ political theory is so foreign to Americans today--even to scholars and teachers of American history and politics--that the task of uncovering the truth about the Founding is like unearthing something from a “deep and long-forgotten pit.”

This course goes about this task by examining the actual words and actions of the Founders, and it brings their theory to life by comparing it to the dominant views of contemporary America.

The result is an exciting and often surprising account of how the Founders established a free government in America and what they thought was necessary to sustain it.

As you know, our online courses are free. That’s why we depend on the generosity of people like you to help us share the course with as many people as possible.

Would you consider making a tax-deductible donation to help promote this course?

And now, for any donation of $100 or more, you will receive this beautiful DVD box set for **“The Real American Founding: A Conversation,”** by clicking the link below.

[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/real-american-founding-dvd](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38156%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=FT0VX10do%2f6RsUcajgfL8w&sysid=1)

I hope you’ll join us in this fun and important work of sharing the truth about the American Founding with your fellow Americans.

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, ’09
Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

---

Your America News
---

name: John Pierce
subject: J6: “Thousands more” to be arrested . . .
body:
So far, more than 1,200 Americans have been arrested. Through my organization, the National Constitutional Law Union (NCLU), I’m currently representing 30 of these defendants.

| | | [](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zo5a7vn32c23h1fsh5arv0q89uh2aap4shgtgd9g_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgmcob1cdh2bs)
Dear Patriot,

[](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z7ie7k7b76v7p85qv8klm9sevdhdhalv31f00s2o_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgmcob1cdh2bs) The U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia recently stood behind a podium and announced the DOJ planned to charge "thousands" more Americans who were present during the January 6th protests.

So far, more than 1,200 Americans have been arrested. Through my organization, the National Constitutional Law Union (NCLU), **I 'm currently representing 30 of these defendants.**

My trial schedule is PACKED, and **[I need your continued support](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z711u2m2ktujkj049jlofu23f6c94ms5q0gon1tg_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgmcob1cdh2bs).**

My name is John Pierce. After my tour in the United States Army, I pursued a law degree at Harvard University. I was also one of the few conservative editors of the _Harvard Law Review_ (after Obama served in that role).

We need TOUGH and relentless warriors who defend the Constitution, and that's why I gave up my lucrative law practice to pursue Constitutional Law. I never expected this to happen.

In my wildest dreams, I could not have imagined our government rounding up and arresting Americans who attended a political protest.

**During the BLM riots, federal buildings were BURNED, police were assaulted _en masse,_ and God knows how many Americans were hurt. And what happened? The Department of Justice dropped all charges against anyone involved. **

But for the January 6th protesters, our government wants to send a clear message: **Stand up to your masters and pay the price!**

I just returned to D.C. for a hearing, and my funds are running low. The cost of airfare and hotels alone for myself, the defendants, and their families are burning through funds.

Criminal litigation is EXPENSIVE, and I'm pushing the limits of my Harvard Law degree to represent these Americans who need our help.

**[Will you help keep us going?](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2zs5cudg0ngce338q08cblsp3inmf51bu9pa4u67g_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgmcob1cdh2bs)**

I have spent countless hours with these defendants and their families. Jobs and homes have been lost. Families have been destroyed. **The DOJ's goal is to terrorize these people to set an example for us all.**

What we see today are the same tactics King George III used before the Revolutionary War started. The British Crown used "writs of assistance" to carry out wide-ranging searches and arrests of anyone for anything. The tactic inspired James Madison to write the Fourth Amendment to our Constitution to protect our privacy.

Today, our government uses high-tech methods to do the same, tracking down anyone who stepped foot on Capitol grounds on January 6th and then using "anonymous" tips to determine their crimes.

Just as King George III abused his power to quell a rebellion among the colonists, our government is abusing its power to do the same.

It's 1776 all over again, and the defendants I represent are the last, best hope that our nation can continue to trust in our Constitution to keep our government in check.

My fight for these men and women isn't just about keeping them out of jail; it's about preserving the framework of our Republic. **[Please help me keep fighting](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z6gjjfturr64k0u0drjrs5otc9tui7iuo3g9hggg_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgmcob1cdh2bs).**

Over the next three weeks, I must raise $58,000 to cover our expenses. Travel, hotels, paralegals, researchers, and assisting attorneys all need to be paid. My credit cards are MAXED out, and I'm relying on you and a handful of other patriots to help. **[Will you chip in today?](http://e.icyminews.com/rd/9z2z0jg9abvqeuuub1qtru02hqvt6jsgfoo87cfrtb0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpgmcob1cdh2bs)**

Every dollar helps and is greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your time, support, and prayers.

In Vigilance,

John M. Pierce
Chairman and Founder
National Constitutional Law Union

# [Martin Luther King, Jr. tried to warn us](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=140695982&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQwNjk1OTgyLCJpYXQiOjE3MDUzMjU4NTQsImV4cCI6MTcwNzkxNzg1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0._Wn8WoPzvnhpreDVR5FgJo7puH6CH7RV1sT1y9SeB9o)

---
Few figures in U.S. history are as widely recognized as the late Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. But while every American has heard his name, and most celebrate today's annual holiday commemorated in his honor, disturbingly [few realize the profundity of his remarkable legacy](https://substack.com/redirect/51f4b1e6-87cb-4a03-913c-7eb9cbea2548?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Even his supporters tend to water it down.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d868c2f9-8350-4295-82be-e583947ff60f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **What MLK stood for**
Dr. King is reduced by most historians to being a leader of the civil rights movement, when in fact he was a visionary whose contributions to America stretched well beyond any single set of issues. Beyond serving as a spokesperson and standard bearer for a movement seeking equal rights long guaranteed under the Constitution, he also emerged as a leading champion of the poor, and a notorious critic of America's military industrial corruption and imperialism.
Are you learning anything from this post? You can help your friends learn, too, by sharing it with them.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=140695982&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQwNjk1OTgyLCJpYXQiOjE3MDUzMjU4NTQsImV4cCI6MTcwNzkxNzg1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0._Wn8WoPzvnhpreDVR5FgJo7puH6CH7RV1sT1y9SeB9o)
In addition to risking arrest and state violence on behalf of people denied basic rights for centuries, Dr. King also embraced even more severe risks by publicly speaking out against Washington's war on Vietnam. By doing so, he joined dissidents from Muhammad Ali to [Daniel Ellsberg](https://substack.com/redirect/28eb3d95-9517-45ad-87b6-abd763d6daa0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who also challenged the war machine at great cost to themselves and their families.
King's actions exemplified a willingness to share burdens, to stand in non-violent solidarity with vulnerable people despite the risks that came with doing so. [His words](https://substack.com/redirect/e5b7ae42-e592-4ff9-bd4e-0abd901386e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) expressed the moral philosophy that drove him to such prolific action:
> "I speak as a child of God and brother to the suffering poor of Vietnam. I speak for those whose land is being laid waste, whose homes are being destroyed, whose culture is being subverted. I speak for the poor of America, who are paying the double price of smashed hopes at home and death and corruption in Vietnam. I speak as a citizen of the world, for the world as it stands aghast at the path we have taken. I speak as one who loves America, to the leaders of our own nation: The great initiative in this war is ours; the initiative to stop it must be ours."
A [letter that Dr. King wrote](https://substack.com/redirect/1ab25adb-e1bd-4287-ac8c-e0f84df86044?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) while detained in a jail in Alabama came to represent among the most crucial texts in American political history. It explained the moral bankruptcy of observing injustice without stepping forward to challenge it. That so many Americans managed to ignore those crucial lessons is a tragic shame.
In time, it would lead to another.
Dr. King explained a [theory of justice defined by a recognition of intersections](https://substack.com/redirect/bae7da8d-c443-48d4-a8b2-4c8e9e53d8ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and how the whole can grow worse than its sordid parts. Observing the corruption that enabled white supremacy in his era, he described militarism and capitalism as evils intersecting--enabling, and also enabled by--racism.
While his words reflected on America at a particular point in time, Dr. King's greatest legacy may ultimately prove to be timeless. It has also been ironically lost on most of his supporters, who lionize the man and his memory, but overlook how his words apply to our world today.
### **What MLK warned us about**
Did MLK envision specifically how the future might grow besieged by a combination of the evils that he identified? He didn't specifically describe the global climate crisis, but he did specifically address each of the precursors that contrived to engineer it over the decades following his assassination.
He warned America about the intersections between [the three social factors most responsible for the climate catastrophe](https://substack.com/redirect/c03664ff-775d-46b0-aa8e-d8eff97e0eaf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that has come to threaten all life on Earth: capitalism, militarism, and racism.
Want to keep learning? Subscribe! Subscriptions are free, while paid subscriptions help support and expand my work.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..cr_FPjhq52Ky9LeH6tRp7scCMItXcAMR1Hr3Kzzlg1o?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=140695982)
Capitalism plays an obvious role, particularly in driving corporate resource extraction. How much oil might remain in the ground today without the profit motive enticing capitalists for the past two centuries to sell every drop they could extract?
Militarism not only [consumes](https://substack.com/redirect/7e73a771-0c50-42bd-a1e9-5bf23f77ef4b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) fossil fuels, but also enables the corporate resource extraction sought by capitalists, particularly when local governments object to foreign exploitation. The [history of Iran in 1953](https://substack.com/redirect/c55caa75-30e1-41a5-837e-a29b2a6ec776?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) offers a case study that is both illustrative and frighteningly prescient.
Tehran antagonized Washington by nationalizing its oil fields in order to prevent foreign exploitation. A [retaliatory CIA coup](https://substack.com/redirect/5cb8181e-4afc-4874-aa41-224d34e5c0ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) proved successful, in the sense that it removed a democratically elected leader and replaced him with a puppet more friendly to Wall Street. But when that dictator's brutality inspired [a revolution 25 years later](https://substack.com/redirect/982311df-244d-453b-a1c8-72c68a86c114?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), in 1979, the ultimate beneficiaries were right-wing theocrats who describe America to this day as "the Great Satan."
The CIA's machinations in Iran in 1953 demonstrated this pattern of coups serving Wall Street. In that respect, it offers an apt illustration of the militarism that MLK decried.
But it was also historically devastating, establishing a pattern while he was alive that Washington continued to [repeat over and over again](https://substack.com/redirect/c9b0dbb0-0294-4152-b553-c30b4e3337df?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--from Latin America to Southeast Asia to the Middle East--over the ensuing decades, long after MLK was taken from us.
Would your friends appreciate learning about Dr. King's radical legacy?
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=140695982&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTQwNjk1OTgyLCJpYXQiOjE3MDUzMjU4NTQsImV4cCI6MTcwNzkxNzg1NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0._Wn8WoPzvnhpreDVR5FgJo7puH6CH7RV1sT1y9SeB9o)
Finally, racism plays the role of both cause and effect. America could not wage wars for profit without denigrating the people down range from the Pentagon's ordnance. And they ultimately pay the greatest price for its corruption.
From the "gooks" [murdered by the millions](https://substack.com/redirect/11029ede-c166-462f-8076-475d09565397?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Vietnam, Cambodia, and Laos to the "towelheads" subjected to military occupation in Iraq and Afghanistan a generation later, the Pentagon's targets share a disturbing pattern of being dehumanized by Americans.
It's a pattern from which other countries have learned well. When Israel escalated its genocidal violence in Gaza last fall, many authorities--including military and elected officials--[explicitly referred to Palestinians](https://substack.com/redirect/bef33c8e-8cd4-448f-94ff-8a5ac6779930?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as animals.
### **What King confronted**
Most Americans who casually celebrate a holiday today forget the dramatic risks faced by Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Some might remember how authorities released dogs on marchers at the Edmund Pettus bridge in Selma, Alabama in 1965. Some might recall the beatings that MLK endured by police, his time in jail, or the death threats targeting him & his family.
But most forget that he was a target of a [thoroughly documented FBI plot](https://substack.com/redirect/22ee475b-5ad3-4865-b96a-17a40b9ebf72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that continued for years. It included not only invasive surveillance, but also [blackmail](https://substack.com/redirect/7fda6517-6bda-41a8-92f7-582de7890dac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) aimed to drive him to an early grave.
What does it reflect when federal government agencies threaten and intimidate peaceful people in retaliation for raising their voices? It suggests that America's commitment to First Amendment principles--and the democracy they were [designed to protect](https://substack.com/redirect/6528534f-eb7d-4b03-a75c-2708939eaa8e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--was never as robust as we might wish.
It suggests that the crisis confronting democracy in America started long before the corporate political parties more recently decided to [abandon primary elections](https://substack.com/redirect/ac68270b-4749-4ae3-b2c5-43ae9b197a10?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), presidential debates, and the last remaining vestige of popular control over our government.
What MLK confronted was not only the racism long embraced by the South, but also the corruption of northern institutions dedicated to preserving capital, even if it meant embracing Jim Crow. And while his contemporary successors learn of his struggle through the lens of its widely celebrated victories, few remember [how many goals of the civil rights movement were never satisfied](https://substack.com/redirect/03aa73a6-2192-40f8-b31c-640cad025854?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--or how the few victories it did secure in his era have eroded in the time since then.
### **What would MLK do today? **
Celebrating today's holiday sincerely would require not only remembering Dr. King, what he stood for, and what he endured, but also taking some action to challenge injustice inspired by his example. There seems no more important question to ask ourselves on this holiday than what he would do, were he still alive.
I can't claim to predict where this life might have led Dr. King had he been allowed the opportunity to enjoy it. But, given his words, his actions, and his legacy, I can at least confidently predict one thing he would _not_ be doing: supporting the fraudulent [co-optation of Black America](https://substack.com/redirect/de1f223c-e3a5-4416-866b-bf69e9c387ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on which the corporate Democratic Party relies.
Dr. King would find little common ground with the Congressional Black Caucus, or the corporate leadership of the party that it plays a crucial role in enabling. He would not be giving paid speeches to Wall Street banks. And he would not be lavishing praise on the first Black president for riding his coattails to a political victory and then abandoning everything that he stood for. He certainly would not welcome that president [empowering](https://substack.com/redirect/87f65ff5-a0bd-4eb5-b0da-90cda509ecf6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the agency that [vilified](https://substack.com/redirect/22ee475b-5ad3-4865-b96a-17a40b9ebf72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) him from public accountability for its [latest](https://substack.com/redirect/48931540-a448-4d34-bf44-18103359f54f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) generation of civil liberties violations.
Assessing the voices active in American politics and culture today, there is one who not only evokes Dr. King routinely, but also stands firmly in his footsteps as a radical agitator and thorn in the side of the bipartisan establishment. He cites a prophetic tradition of inspired resistance to oppression to the very same social forces that Dr. King described as "intersecting evils."
His name is [Dr. Cornel West](https://substack.com/redirect/a04737a5-4135-49dc-8ff6-0f708078ef03?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). And unlike either Biden or Trump, he's willing to show up in public and [defend his ideas](https://substack.com/redirect/ac68270b-4749-4ae3-b2c5-43ae9b197a10?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
That willingness to boldly announce and defend our ideas might be the most important lesson that we today can learn from Dr. King's largely forgotten legacy.
Paid supporters can access video from a September 2019 speech at the Youth Climate Strike in San Jose. In addition to citing Dr. King, I explained how his prescient formulation of the intersecting evils applies to the global climate crisis. Applying his intersectional approach, I also highlight why any meaningful solution for climate justice must ultimately include more than the radical changes to energy policy on which climate justice organizations are tragically and myopically focused...

America lionized MLK as a national hero while managing to obscure his legacy | | | Jan 15| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | Few figures in U.S. history are as widely recognized as the late Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. But while every American has heard his name, and most celebrate today's annual holiday commemorated in his honor, disturbingly . Even his supporters tend to water it down. | | ---|---|--- What MLK stood for Dr. King is reduced by most historians to being a leader of the civil rights movement, when in fact he was a visionary whose contributions to America stretched well beyond any single set of issues. Beyond serving as a spokesperson and standard bearer for a movement seeking equal rights long guaranteed under the Constitution, he also emerged as a leading champion of the poor, and a notorious critic of America's military industrial corruption and imperialism. Are you learning anything from this post? You can help your friends learn, too, by sharing it with them. In addition to risking arrest and state violence on behalf of people denied basic rights for centuries, Dr. King also embraced even more severe risks by publicly speaking out against Washington's war on Vietnam. By doing so, he joined dissidents from Muhammad Ali to who also challenged the war machine at great cost to themselves and their families. King's actions exemplified a willingness to share burdens, to stand in non-violent solidarity with vulnerable people despite the risks that came with doing so. expressed the moral philosophy that drove him to such prolific action: "I speak as a child of God and brother to the suffering poor of Vietnam. I speak for those whose land is being laid waste, whose homes are being destroyed, whose culture is being subverted. I speak for the poor of America, who are paying the double price of smashed hopes at home and death and corruption in Vietnam. I speak as a citizen of the world, for the world as it stands aghast at the path we have taken. I speak as one who loves America, to the leaders of our own nation: The great initiative in this war is ours; the initiative to stop it must be ours." A while detained in a jail in Alabama came to represent among the most crucial texts in American political history. It explained the moral bankruptcy of observing injustice without stepping forward to challenge it. That so many Americans managed to ignore those crucial lessons is a tragic shame. In time, it would lead to another. Dr. King explained a and how the whole can grow worse than its sordid parts. Observing the corruption that enabled white supremacy in his era, he described militarism and capitalism as evils intersecting--enabling, and also enabled by--racism. While his words reflected on America at a particular point in time, Dr. King's greatest legacy may ultimately prove to be timeless. It has also been ironically lost on most of his supporters, who lionize the man and his memory, but overlook how his words apply to our world today. What MLK warned us about Did MLK envision specifically how the future might grow besieged by a combination of the evils that he identified? He didn't specifically describe the global climate crisis, but he did specifically address each of the precursors that contrived to engineer it over the decades following his assassination. He warned America about the intersections between that has come to threaten all life on Earth: capitalism, militarism, and racism. Want to keep learning? Subscribe! Subscriptions are free, while paid subscriptions help support and expand my work. Capitalism plays an obvious role, particularly in driving corporate resource extraction. How much oil might remain in the ground today without the profit motive enticing capitalists for the past two centuries to sell every drop they could extract? Militarism not only fossil fuels, but also enables the corporate resource extraction sought by capitalists, particularly when local governments object to foreign exploitation. The offers a case study that is both illustrative and frighteningly prescient. Tehran antagonized Washington by nationalizing its oil fields in order to prevent foreign exploitation. A proved successful, in the sense that it removed a democratically elected leader and replaced him with a puppet more friendly to Wall Street. But when that dictator's brutality inspired , in 1979, the ultimate beneficiaries were right-wing theocrats who describe America to this day as "the Great Satan." The CIA's machinations in Iran in 1953 demonstrated this pattern of coups serving Wall Street. In that respect, it offers an apt illustration of the militarism that MLK decried. But it was also historically devastating, establishing a pattern while he was alive that Washington continued to Latin America to Southeast Asia to the Middle East--over the ensuing decades, long after MLK was taken from us. Would your friends appreciate learning about Dr. King's radical legacy? Finally, racism plays the role of both cause and effect. America could not wage wars for profit without denigrating the people down range from the Pentagon's ordnance. And they ultimately pay the greatest price for its corruption. From the "gooks" in Vietnam, Cambodia, and Laos to the "towelheads" subjected to military occupation in Iraq and Afghanistan a generation later, the Pentagon's targets share a disturbing pattern of being dehumanized by Americans. It's a pattern from which other countries have learned well. When Israel escalated its genocidal violence in Gaza last fall, many authorities--including military and elected officials-- as animals. What King confronted Most Americans who casually celebrate a holiday today forget the dramatic risks faced by Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. Some might remember how authorities released dogs on marchers at the Edmund Pettus bridge in Selma, Alabama in 1965. Some might recall the beatings that MLK endured by police, his time in jail, or the death threats targeting him & his family. But most forget that he was a target of a that continued for years. It included not only invasive surveillance, but also aimed to drive him to an early grave. What does it reflect when federal government agencies threaten and intimidate peaceful people in retaliation for raising their voices? It suggests that America's commitment to First Amendment principles--and the democracy they were never as robust as we might wish. It suggests that the crisis confronting democracy in America started long before the corporate political parties more recently decided to , presidential debates, and the last remaining vestige of popular control over our government. What MLK confronted was not only the racism long embraced by the South, but also the corruption of northern institutions dedicated to preserving capital, even if it meant embracing Jim Crow. And while his contemporary successors learn of his struggle through the lens of its widely celebrated victories, few remember how the few victories it did secure in his era have eroded in the time since then. What would MLK do today? Celebrating today's holiday sincerely would require not only remembering Dr. King, what he stood for, and what he endured, but also taking some action to challenge injustice inspired by his example. There seems no more important question to ask ourselves on this holiday than what he would do, were he still alive. I can't claim to predict where this life might have led Dr. King had he been allowed the opportunity to enjoy it. But, given his words, his actions, and his legacy, I can at least confidently predict one thing he would not be doing: supporting the fraudulent on which the corporate Democratic Party relies. Dr. King would find little common ground with the Congressional Black Caucus, or the corporate leadership of the party that it plays a crucial role in enabling. He would not be giving paid speeches to Wall Street banks. And he would not be lavishing praise on the first Black president for riding his coattails to a political victory and then abandoning everything that he stood for. He certainly would not welcome that president the agency that him from public accountability for its generation of civil liberties violations. Assessing the voices active in American politics and culture today, there is one who not only evokes Dr. King routinely, but also stands firmly in his footsteps as a radical agitator and thorn in the side of the bipartisan establishment. He cites a prophetic tradition of inspired resistance to oppression to the very same social forces that Dr. King described as "intersecting evils." His name is . And unlike either Biden or Trump, he's willing to show up in public and . That willingness to boldly announce and defend our ideas might be the most important lesson that we today can learn from Dr. King's largely forgotten legacy. In addition to citing Dr. King, I explained how his prescient formulation of the intersecting evils applies to the global climate crisis. Applying his intersectional approach, I also highlight why any meaningful solution for climate justice must ultimately include more than the radical changes to energy policy on which climate justice organizations are tragically and myopically focused... body:
Spot us at these events in honor of Dr. King.

[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/77103590/449390193/-1739567893?refcode=EM_C_240115king_N_TN_90DO_12_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzkwOTIwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI0OTMyNTU3LWFjYjMtZWUxMS1iZWExLTAwMjI0ODIyMzdkYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=aeWy4i5ggv7u3AF8wCEU5iDSksV_RXZbdKw4FIxBBlc=)
| Fellow Activist,

Today, the New Pennsylvania Project and the affiliated New PA Project Education Fund are commemorating the life and work of Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

In his honor, you can find our team out around PA at the following events:

✊🏾 **Philly:** Broad St. Ministries and African American History, Constitution Museums

✊🏾 **Bucks/MontCo:** MLK Peace & Social Justice Summit

✊🏾 **Chester/Delaware:** Reenactment March in Coatesville

✊🏾 **Lehigh/Poconos:** Allentown Art Museum and St. James Church service

✊🏾 **Berks/Lancaster:** Women’s Empowerment and Park Clean-up

✊🏾 **Capital Region:** Bread of Life

✊🏾 **Allegheny:** Kelly Strayhorn Theater - Justice in Action and Kelly Strayhorn Theater - Let Freedom Sing

Effectively organizing in our community means that we cannot just show up in September or October of election year to tell folks to vote. We have to build authentic and lasting connections within our communities year-round. We are honored to be celebrating the life and legacy of Dr. King, and Mrs. Coretta Scott King too, today and every day by showing up around PA and ensuring that everyone has what they need to make their voices heard.

As we head into another critical election year, we are thankful to live in a country where we can vote and have a chance to be a part of the governmental process. Dr. King spoke about this many times – communities of color must have elected leaders who will govern in their interests and make the promise of our democracy real for everyone.

Thank you for joining us in this fight, and happy Martin Luther King Jr. Day!

In solidarity,

Team NPP | __The New Pennsylvania Project (NPP) is a voting rights organization with a year-round primary focus on voter registration, civic education, and mobilization. NPP centers historically disenfranchised and often neglected Black, Indigenous and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth in our work. Through civic engagement, we ensure all eligible voters feel compelled to exercise their freedom to vote in the Commonwealth.__ __**[DONATE MONTHLY](https://click.everyaction.com/k/77103593/449390195/-1739567893?refcode=EM_C_240115king_N_TN_90DO_12_NG_N_BIO&recurring=auto&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVjAwMS8xLzkwOTIwIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjI0OTMyNTU3LWFjYjMtZWUxMS1iZWExLTAwMjI0ODIyMzdkYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=aeWy4i5ggv7u3AF8wCEU5iDSksV_RXZbdKw4FIxBBlc=)**__
