name: Officer Harry Dunn (ret.)
subject: Three years ago, I defended the U.S. Capitol from violent insurrectionists. Now, I'm the Democrat running for Congress in MD-03 and I hope you’ll become a Founding Donor of my campaign.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Forward Blue and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
# [](https://links.e.forwardblue.)
[](https://links.e.forwardblue.)
Hey folks, it's Harry Dunn here, one of the Capitol police officers who defended the U.S. Capitol against MAGA insurrectionists just three years ago and testified in front of the January 6th Committee.
I've got an important announcement: **I 'm hoping to make my return to DC - but not as a Capitol police officer. I'm officially running to serve Maryland's 3rd Congressional district in the U.S. House.**
[_**I 'm counting on your support to get there. Can you rush $10 or more split with Forward Blue to become a Founding Donor of my campaign?**_](https://links.e.forwardblue.net/els/v2/6-)
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Donate $10 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.) [Donate $24 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.) [Donate $50 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.net/els/v2/zA42c~~) [Donate $100 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.net/els/v2/0zeVCzzjL_t~) [Donate $250 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.) [Other amount ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.)
---
Here's the thing folks, I _never_ thought I would run for office. Growing up, I wanted to be just like my dad, who was an athlete and a U.S. Air Force Veteran. I played football and earned an athletic scholarship that sent me to college. (_I was pretty good, but being 6-foot-7 sure didn 't hurt!_)
But wearing a football uniform taught me something special: it gave me purpose and made me feel a part of something bigger than myself.
So when I put on my Capitol police uniform for the first time over 15 years ago to protect our nation's Capitol, _it felt right_.
But I never thought I'd see the day that our democracy would be attacked by Donald Trump and an army of insurrectionists that HE directed to the Capitol.
I couldn't have been any closer to the chaos of January 6. I put my life on the line during the violence and destruction that Trump encouraged. So trust and believe when I tell you: _**we cannot allow our democracy to be in the hands of these MAGA-extremists.**_
I fought for our democracy and our Capitol once as Officer Dunn, and I'll do it again as Congressman Dunn.
**[_Please, can you chip in $10 or $24 to become a Founding Donor of my campaign? I seriously appreciate any help you can give today >>_](https://links.e.forwardblue.)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Donate $10 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.) [Donate $24 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.) [Donate $50 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.net/els/v2/zA42c~~) [Donate $100 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.net/els/v2/0zeVCzzjL_t~) [Donate $250 ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.) [Other amount ›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.)
---
Thank you for reading,
Harry Dunn

We work tirelessly to ensure that every eligible voter has an opportunity to cast their ballot. As we build on our momentum toward Election Day, there is no room to slow down. We are full speed ahead to push for a progressive victory! **[Donate Monthly›››](https://links.e.forwardblue.net/els/v2/exPdQkkR~)**
---
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com). Email is the most important way we keep in touch with supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://links.e.forwardblue.)
* [Unsubscribe](https://links.e.forwardblue.)

To give by check:
Forward Blue

Somers Point, NJ 08244
Email us: [info@forwardblue.org](mailto:info@forwardblue.org)

name: Angela Alsobrooks
subject: Dr. King’s message is more important than ever
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
|
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2092?t=1&akid=930%2E52803%2EJ7orxN)

| | With our country polarized and at a crossroads about our national identity, Dr. King’s message of love, nonviolence, and justice is more important now than ever before, Peter. He taught us that while we may disagree, there’s far more that connects us than divides us. But he also taught us that we must not waver in the face of injustice — that we must face down inequity and bigotry, as well as those who wield them as weapons. We must be _relentless_ in our quest for social justice and the common good. His vision for a world of inclusion, fairness, and equal opportunity is one we’re still trying to attain, but it’s worth fighting for. Because as he reminds us, _“the arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends toward justice.”_ With love and empathy, Angela Alsobrooks [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2570?t=7&akid=930%2E52803%2EJ7orxN)

---

Friend,
Much ink has been spilled attempting to make profound statements on Dr. King's legacy, and I want to be careful not to shoehorn a personal or political agenda on it here.
Instead, I wanted to express one way in which MLK's words and thoughts and feelings still resonate today--and how even for a young white man from rural Appalachia, like me, there is inspiration to be found from Dr. King's philosophy of peace and empathy in the face of conflict and struggle.
"Let us not seek to satisfy our thirst for freedom by drinking from the cup of bitterness and hatred. We must forever conduct our struggle on the high plain of dignity and discipline." -- Martin Luther King, Jr., "I Have a Dream," Aug. 28, 1963
It is too easy in politics to stereotype, ignore, and belittle each other. Even in this historically polarized time, I know that there is hope in the long-term project of bringing more folks together around what unites us rather than what divides us.
Bitterness and hatred are easy; to hate requires only to give up on seeing others as we wish to be seen. Empathy and understanding require work, and therefore discipline.
In Dr. King's memory, I seek to conduct myself in this campaign, and in my life generally, with dignity and discipline, working to do my best to satisfy our "thirst for freedom" with love and never hatred.
Though we may fall short, these are the ideals toward which I believe we can and must strive as Americans and as people, repairing our union and our humanity for a better future.
I hope you have a restful and reflective Martin Luther King, Jr. Day.

Sincerely,
-Jerrad
---

name: Becca Balint
subject: Dangerous unselfishness
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont
body:
Today is Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day – a day to reflect on the life and legacy of Dr. King.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77115101/449423273/966991095?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMTdiMjJlOS1iOGIzLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VjwpYE317lomE5SbVqyo7mLLVpsLDUPySnB_XLyNZ7M=&emci=5ea78263-83b1-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=217b22e9-b8b3-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=43402272) | Dear Peter, Today is Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day – a day to reflect on the life and legacy of Dr. King. During the final speech Dr. King gave the day before his assassination in Memphis, he asked his allies and supporters to adopt a mindset of _“dangerous unselfishness.”_ To me, what this means is that in the fight for progress, we must put the needs of others above our own fears, be courageous and empathetic in our daily interactions, and keep our sights set on battling injustice in all of its forms – whether or not we are personally impacted by those injustices. **Like Dr. King, we must engage in meaningful conversations with our neighbors and friends about racial injustice. ** In a state like Vermont that is overwhelmingly white, it is imperative that we have uncomfortable conversations, hold our friends and acquaintances accountable for their behavior and impact, and speak out in our communities about the injustices we see. I hope you’ll join me in taking a moment today to reflect on how you can use your privilege to speak up about injustice in your community. Confronting the injustices in our communities and country at large is up to every single one of us. Yours in progress, — Becca
---

* * *

name: Arizona Democratic Party
subject: Honoring Dr. King’s commitment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Arizona Democrats
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/azd-em-header [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/azd-em-header]
Hi there,

Today, we’re honoring the extraordinary life and valuable work of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

Dr. King’s powerful commitment to fighting for justice for all and a future free of prejudice is the reason we have many of the rights we enjoy today. But the fight isn’t over. Oppression still exists in many forms in this country, but together, we’re determined to secure justice and progress.

For decades, extremist Republicans have constantly threatened and attacked the rights of marginalized communities. Recently, they’ve become hell-bent on undermining our democracy and stripping millions of Americans of what Dr. King worked so hard to secure: the right to vote.

Right here in Arizona, in 2022, Republicans carried out fraudulent voter-roll purges that put thousands of eligible voters’ registration status in jeopardy. Dozens of lawsuits have been filed here against voter suppression laws that target communities of color, new citizens, those who don’t speak English, and Native Americans.

In the spirit of Dr. King, we’re fighting back.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/azd-em-jan24 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/azd-em-jan24]

In 2023, Arizona Democratic Party’s organizers registered hundreds of Arizonans from all walks of life to vote. 2024 is an election year — that means we’re going to need boots on the ground across the state to register even more voters and set a record for voter turnout.

It won’t be an easy fight, but we can do it together. Every Arizonan and every American deserves to have their voice heard and their vote counted.

Thank you,

— Arizona Democratic Party
Arizona Democratic Party
PO Box 36123
Phoenix, AZ 85067-6123
United States
info@azdem.org [info@azdem.org]

name: Michael B. Moore
subject: On this Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Michael B
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77116511/449427169/-251759529?refcode=header&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQ1ODUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjIyZjNiZGUtYmFiMy1lZTExLWJlYTEtMDAyMjQ4MjIzN2RhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=0MuIrvSWVrFNg2MKyMwsBYE7JkExNjIC2Gp5CXTP3NI=) | Peter – like so many others – Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. is a hero of mine.

In the face of racism and segregation, he stood up for the America that he knew we deserved to live in – one that prioritizes equity and justice for all. Dr. King's tirelessness was a driving force that helped end the Jim Crow era in our country and inspired the Voting Rights Act of 1965.

Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was a changemaker, and it's our responsibility to continue his legacy through public service.

So today, I have two short asks of you: First, volunteer your time at a food drive, public park cleanup, voter registration session, or an organization of your choice. Your community will thank you. Second, I'm reminded of a quote of Dr. King's that has been a true north of mine: _"Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that."_ So, I'm asking you to **lead with light for history tells us it's the only way positive change to come about.** Wishing you a day full of service to your neighbors, \- Michael B. Moore [MEET MICHAEL](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77116512/449427170/1446694319?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2024.01.15_B1_MLKJr.Day_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQ1ODUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjIyZjNiZGUtYmFiMy1lZTExLWJlYTEtMDAyMjQ4MjIzN2RhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=0MuIrvSWVrFNg2MKyMwsBYE7JkExNjIC2Gp5CXTP3NI=&emci=abc2efd7-27af-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=b22f3bde-bab3-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=14781535) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77116513/449427171/-251759529?refcode=footer&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQ1ODUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjIyZjNiZGUtYmFiMy1lZTExLWJlYTEtMDAyMjQ4MjIzN2RhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=0MuIrvSWVrFNg2MKyMwsBYE7JkExNjIC2Gp5CXTP3NI=)

clean_body: = | Peter – like so many others – Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. is a hero of mine. In the face of racism and segregation, he stood up for the America that he knew we deserved to live in – one that prioritizes equity and justice for all. Dr. King's tirelessness was a driving force that helped end the Jim Crow era in our country and inspired the Voting Rights Act of 1965. Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was a changemaker, and it's our responsibility to continue his legacy through public service. So today, I have two short asks of you: First, volunteer your time at a food drive, public park cleanup, voter registration session, or an organization of your choice. Your community will thank you. Second, I'm reminded of a quote of Dr. King's that has been a true north of mine: "Darkness cannot drive out darkness; only light can do that." So, I'm asking you to lead with light for history tells us it's the only way positive change to come about. Wishing you a day full of service to your neighbors, \- Michael B. Moore = | | Paid for by Michael B. Moore for South Carolina Michael B. name: Election Day Alert [via Team Haley]
subject: ARE YOU READY?
body:
IT’S CAUCUS DAY!

| |
---
| | | | [](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38153%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=NLL5KB001CSOKRPKTYCMAA&sysid=1)
---

Friend, ARE YOU READY?

THIS IS IT.

THIS IS GO TIME.

At 7 PM TONIGHT, Iowans will get out to caucus for Nikki.

We have a **few hours** until the caucuses begin.

That means we have a **few hours** left to amplify Nikki’s message across Iowa.

We have a **few hours** left to change the minds of undecided voters.

We have a **few hours** left to get out the vote and take one step closer to victory.

**[Chip $5 or more NOW to support Nikki and let’s do this!](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38154%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=AVtp4JEcnYtJFmN9DllwHw&sysid=1 "Chip $5 or more NOW to
support Nikki and let’s do this! ")**
---
| | [DONATE](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38165%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=TqPK7XhU9Ja4hxads24s6A&sysid=1)
---

We have been pouring our ALL into this movement over the last 11 months to get to this point.

**NOW IS THE MOMENT** we have worked so hard for.

But we **CANNOT** let the momentum slip with just hours to go until the first votes take place.

_That’s why we need**YOUR** help today. _

We need to finish Iowa **STRONG**. And then immediately after Iowa, we’ve got to keep pounding the pavement because New Hampshire’s primary is just **8 DAYS AWAY!**

**[Would you be willing to donate just $5 or more to fuel our campaign and amplify Nikki’s message during these final hours ahead of the first votes?](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38166%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=t6pCt30u78Qgi5n4Q3A3tw&sysid=1 "Would you be willing to
donate just $5 or more to fuel our campaign and amplify Nikki’s message during
these final hours ahead of the first votes?")**

Please join us now...before it is too late.

You can help determine the outcome of this race and our country’s future.

_**Let’s WIN this!**_

---
| | [DONATE](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38165%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=TqPK7XhU9Ja4hxads24s6A&sysid=1)
---

\- Team Haley

---
| | | | |
---
| [](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38167%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=Wti5I1dibrMaaLQA8Ttjcg&sysid=1 "NikkiHaley_Merch")
---
| | [GET YOUR HAT NOW](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38168%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=Dg4I%2fCv714RS0ZIYL8pBCQ&sysid=1 "Claim Your Mug")
---
---
| | **More About Nikki Haley**

---
| [](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38169%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=U6BTP3ZRqQDru7cxyLYUaQ&sysid=1 "NikkiHaley_Podium")
---
My name is Nikki Haley. I want to tell you a bit about myself and why I’m running for President of the United States.

I’m running for President because my parents came to America more than 50 years ago - an America that was strong and proud, and full of opportunities. I want them to know that America again.

I’m running for President as a military spouse. My husband is deployed and I want him and his military brothers and sisters to know their sacrifice means something - that we do love this country.

I’m running for President for the future of our kids. I have a daughter who just got married and I saw how hard it was for her and her husband to buy a home. I’m doing this for my son who’s a senior in college and shouldn’t have to worry about saying things on a paper that he doesn’t believe in just to get an A.

I’m running for my daughter, my son, and our future generations. I want them to experience the freedom and opportunity that the American Dream has to offer.

_**I’m running for President because we have a country to save! **_

I’ve seen that even on our worst days, we are blessed to live in America. But I also know that under Biden’s leadership, times are tough, our future is slipping, and America can be doing so much better.

**As governor, I was dubbed a “jobs governor”** because I slashed our unemployment rate, brought jobs back to our state, and shepherded in a successful economic recovery.

**At the UN I fought for America,** made sure our allies knew we had their backs and stood up to murderers and dictators to let them know that America was taking names.

I know how to make America strong and proud because I’ve done it before. **And I’ll do it as your next President. **

As President, I’ll revitalize our economy, support small businesses, stop Washington’s spending sprees, protect our border, champion school choice, and save America’s future.

**[Will you join me today and help me save our country?](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38160%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=dKwCME8hlA4%2benYb9YeRvg&sysid=1 "Will you join me today and
help me save our country?")**

---
| | [DONATE](https://click.youramerica.news/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=653%7c38161%7c920805%7c0%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=xHyQcqwEuNYCgRisJMIZaQ&sysid=1 "DONATE")

name: Mike Johnson [Speaker of the House]
subject: You've never seen fire and brimstone until you've gone head-to-head with Trump-hating socialists.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
So you can imagine the immediate wrath I faced when I went on record supporting Donald J. Trump for President.

| |

---
| | | My fellow Americans - this is Speaker Mike Johnson.

_You've never seen fire and brimstone until you've gone head-to-head with Trump-hating socialists. _

So you can imagine the immediate wrath I faced when I went on record supporting Donald J. Trump for President. I was just talking to my friend, Steve Scalise - he faced the same thing last week when he endorsed Trump too.

_**[He and I agree: as strong Pro-Trump Conservatives, we've got to stick together.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zj477lp51625u05umdauvu757ojkkfh3q6medff8_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs "He and I agree: as strong Pro-Trump Conservatives, we’ve got to stick together.")**_

That's because the anti-Trump Media... Young radicals on college campuses... the holier-than-thou elites in Hollywood... Democrat Super PACs... Joe Biden's army of megadonors... They are fully committed to destroying our movement and replacing EVERY LAST ONE OF US with a Liberal Democrat.

But wait, that's not even the half of it. It gets worse:
** Problem #1: ** We have THREE imminent Special Elections and Democrats committed more than five million dollars to install more Liberals.

** Problem #2: ** House Democrats have forty million bucks stashed away to attack pro-Trump Conservatives like us.

** Problem #3: ** Joe Biden just bragged about raising a million bucks in a SINGLE DAY attacking President Trump.
I cannot stress the urgency of the situation enough. Early voting is about to start in the first Special Election AND the Left's fundraising advantage grows by the minute...

_**[So as one of my first moves of the election year, I'm teaming up with my fellow Louisianian, Steve Scalise, to set a goal of raising one million dollars between now and the end of the month.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z5rtfubsi3alllvui6kha7tp6qtq8416p03lt2p0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs "So as one of my first moves of the election year, I’m teaming up with my fellow Louisianian, Steve Scalise, to set a goal of raising one million dollars between now and the end of the month.")**_

I know, I know - that's a big goal. I get that. But that's as low as I could possibly make it to fully fund our campaign operations in every must-win swing state.

If we fail to hit it, the consequences would be unthinkable. We'd have no choice but to: **1) ** SCRAP our get-out-the-vote operations, **2) ** SLASH the budget for our ad campaigns, and **3) ** Throw our digital outreach plans IN THE TRASH CAN.

Nothing - and I mean NOTHING - would be worse for our plans to defend our razor-thin House Majority. **That 's why I'm calling for reinforcements. **

_I know the economy is in bad shape under Joe Biden. So if that 's preventing you from pitching in $100 or $50 - I completely understand. But if you can afford JUST $10 or even $5, I would be grateful beyond measure. _

_**[I only ask everyone reading this to pitch for a few bucks or whatever you can afford.](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zvd78i9qaf97igv2cbgbp5hioerpnugsiu250mt8_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs "I only ask everyone reading this to pitch for a few bucks or whatever you can afford.")**_

When the stakes are this high, that's what will be the difference between saving our Republican Majority and losing to the Trump-hating Democrats. So can I count on you to pitch in SOMETHING?
---
**[](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zgtifkq62gmefq8tpcu95j2jlkflipjgt8g31jn0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs)[**YES >>**](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zjpfitk30vrqfub7ikl1jehni6sjr4angvptblf8_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs)_[**NO >>**](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z20emg4j9rupoj5dvg2n4bs6j4eaf6b6gkrdoqk0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs)**
---
Thanks for everything you do.

In liberty,
Mike Johnson (Speaker of the House)

---
| [](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z7hoadtjjfhi5ti3uusjqrdgo9jt95mao0jqd54o_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs "Mike Johnson")
---

---
| |
* * *
---
| [](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zscpue9psbq79f1kvt1jelfn8qj7sb4huv0r57f0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs "Please take my Majority Leader Survey!")
---

This is Steve Scalise.

As your Majority Leader, I'm respectfully asking you to fill out my _**[MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z1ecpsgmm5li7q33t5b6jm42saa5bed70f3dmn3g_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs "MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY")**_ at your earliest convenience. It's important. I want to have your goals, your concerns, and your priorities in mind before I do anything else.

What would you like me to keep in mind when I'm taking on the Radical Democrats? | | [START MY MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY >>](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z3ve0fnairmf1ioiasrrrkc4rj88mc9enqhgpadg_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim0or4cph2bs "START MY MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY")

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: The fight to protect and expand voting rights
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
#

Peter, a personal note from me today.
As we celebrate Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., I’m thinking about his legacy.
The Civil Rights Act and the Voting Rights Act were passed thanks to the movement he led. Millions enjoy expanded rights that were deferred or restricted for generations because of him. It’s humbling to think about the impact one person can have on our democracy.
We’ll surely see some half-baked posts from folks like Kari Lake and Kyrsten Sinema about how important Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. was today. True.
But here’s the deal:
Kari Lake has fought his legacy at every turn, and her lies, her extremism, her election denial only aim to restrict the rights that King worked towards. Kyrsten Sinema had the opportunity to vote to amend the filibuster and protect voting rights for millions, and she failed.
Worst of all, we’ve had to watch the Supreme Court absolutely eviscerate the Voting Rights Act in the years since the GOP gained a majority. We are all worse off because of it.
It’s grim. But I’m a Marine — I will not back down. I will not stop fighting to protect and expand those rights. So today, especially today, I hope you’re with me.
If you are, can you take a simple act and let me know you have my back in this campaign? I’ll fight on my own if I have to, but we will always go further if we work together.
[**Use this link and add your name to thousands of others who have endorsed my campaign in Arizona.

https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1-15-24-endorse**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/signup/em_endorse_ruben_jan24/?source=em240115-1-30&refcode2=1717.734936.wnblKl&akid=1717%2E734936%2EwnblKl)
Thank you for your support,
Ruben

Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform do not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails like this are a critical tool for our campaign's fundraising and organizing efforts. If you'd like to receive only the most important emails going forward, [tell us at this link](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/signup/fewer_emails/?source=fewer-emails&t=1&akid=1717%2E734936%2EwnblKl). Or, if you need to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=2&akid=1717%2E734936%2EwnblKl). If you would like to support Ruben's campaign for Senate, please consider [making a donation today.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ruben-em-footer?refcode=em-footer&t=3&refcode2=1717_734936_wnblKl&akid=1717%2E734936%2EwnblKl)

name: =?utf-8?Q?Suzanne=20Crouch?=
subject: From The Campaign Trail: MAJOR Updates, Events & Grassroots Wins! 🌟
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Suzanne Crouch
body:
2023 fundraising numbers revealed! Plus, big news on petition signatures. Can you guess how many we turned in last week?

https://www.crouchforindiana.com/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Friends and fellow Hoosiers,

As we surge into 2024, I'm thrilled to share some exciting updates from our campaign! Let's hit the highlights!

** 🚨 Major Campaign Update! 🚨
------------------------------------------------------------

At the beginning of 2023, we set ambitious goals: Raise $2 million and collect 9,000 signatures. I'm thrilled to report we not only met those goals, but exceeded them!
https://crouchforindiana.com/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

** Team Crouch delivers over 9,000 petition signatures!
------------------------------------------------------------
Last week, Team Crouch submitted more than 9,000 petition signatures—double the amount legally required to get my name on the ballot.

THANK YOU to the volunteers, supporters, and Hoosiers who have helped us exceed these goals and more!

** Praying For Our Lawmakers
------------------------------------------------------------

https://www.facebook.com/CrouchForIndiana?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

I was blessed to join hundreds of Hoosiers at the State Capitol on Monday in praying for this legislative session. Please keep our legislators and staff members in your thoughts throughout the session.

A special thanks to the Public Servants Prayer for hosting this meaningful event.

** Law Enforcement Appreciation Day
------------------------------------------------------------

A heartfelt thank you to the officers across Indiana who work tirelessly to keep us safe. It's an honor to stand alongside our law enforcement, traveling the state to meet them, listen to their needs, and ensure they have the support they deserve.
https://www.facebook.com/CrouchForIndiana/posts/pfbid02u4cvckQHAJmyGBx6GyDRNwuFXqEhAShgUmuah3h9UYGEHDx5JsedcfRHZi5ugQXbl?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

** IN THE NEWS: Interview with Indy Politics' (https://indypolitics.org/my-conversation-with-crouch/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) Abdul-Hakim Shabazz (https://indypolitics.org/my-conversation-with-crouch/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
------------------------------------------------------------

We covered key plans like Axe The Tax and tackling the fentanyl crisis. Plus, how I've fought to protect Hoosier values—thwarting China's land grab attempts and making critical votes protecting the unborn.
https://indypolitics.org/my-conversation-with-crouch/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
INDY POLITICS INTERVIEW (https://indypolitics.org/my-conversation-with-crouch/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

** IN THE NEWS: Chinese billionaire secretly purchased 200K acres of US land, sparking outrage (https://nypost.com/2024/01/13/news/chen-tianqiaos-purchase-of-200k-oregon-acres-sparks-outrage/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
------------------------------------------------------------

https://nypost.com/2024/01/13/news/chen-tianqiaos-purchase-of-200k-oregon-acres-sparks-outrage/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

As this story proves, the threat from Chinese Communist land grabbers is real.

As governor, I'll continue to protect Hoosier farmers and our national security from foreign aggression.
ARTICLE (https://nypost.com/2024/01/13/news/chen-tianqiaos-purchase-of-200k-oregon-acres-sparks-outrage/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

** Have you seen my 2 new TV ads?
------------------------------------------------------------

If not, you can check them out here (https://crouchforindiana.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=db6332b918877c0167cdddb48&id=b5468f3304&e=2451eaeeac&utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) and here (https://crouchforindiana.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=db6332b918877c0167cdddb48&id=4cde7291fd&e=2451eaeeac&utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) !

The new ads detail my priorities as governor and how I️ have fought to prevent the Chinese Communist Party and its leaders from acquiring land in Indiana.
Campaign Ad 1: Conservative Fighter (https://vimeo.com/899679899?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
Campaign Ad 2: 'Open Your Eyes' (https://vimeo.com/899678221?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

** ICYMI: Endorsements (https://crouchforindiana.com/endorsements/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
------------------------------------------------------------

(https://crouchforindiana.com/endorsements/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=54058e0769-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_01_09_03_54&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-54058e0769-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

------------------------------------------------------------

As we move forward, your continued support is crucial. Together, we can make history and shape a better future for all Hoosiers. Stay tuned for more updates, and let's keep the momentum going!

Onward and upward,

clean_body: 2023 fundraising numbers revealed! Plus, big news on petition signatures. Can you guess how many we turned in last week? Friends and fellow Hoosiers, As we surge into 2024, I'm thrilled to share some exciting updates from our campaign! Let's hit the highlights! 🚨 Major Campaign Update! 🚨 At the beginning of 2023, we set ambitious goals: Raise $2 million and collect 9,000 signatures. I'm thrilled to report we not only met those goals, but exceeded them! Team Crouch delivers over 9,000 petition signatures! Last week, Team Crouch submitted more than 9,000 petition signatures—double the amount legally required to get my name on the ballot. THANK YOU to the volunteers, supporters, and Hoosiers who have helped us exceed these goals and more! Praying For Our Lawmakers I was blessed to join hundreds of Hoosiers at the State Capitol on Monday in praying for this legislative session. Please keep our legislators and staff members in your thoughts throughout the session. A special thanks to the Public Servants Prayer for hosting this meaningful event. Law Enforcement Appreciation Day A heartfelt thank you to the officers across Indiana who work tirelessly to keep us safe. It's an honor to stand alongside our law enforcement, traveling the state to meet them, listen to their needs, and ensure they have the support they deserve. IN THE NEWS: Interview with Indy Politics' () Abdul-Hakim Shabazz () We covered key plans like Axe The Tax and tackling the fentanyl crisis. Plus, how I've fought to protect Hoosier values—thwarting China's land grab attempts and making critical votes protecting the unborn. INDY POLITICS INTERVIEW () IN THE NEWS: Chinese billionaire secretly purchased 200K acres of US land, sparking outrage () As this story proves, the threat from Chinese Communist land grabbers is real. As governor, I'll continue to protect Hoosier farmers and our national security from foreign aggression. ARTICLE () Have you seen my 2 new TV ads? If not, you can check them out here () and here () ! The new ads detail my priorities as governor and how I️ have fought to prevent the Chinese Communist Party and its leaders from acquiring land in Indiana. Campaign Ad 1: Conservative Fighter () Campaign Ad 2: 'Open Your Eyes' () ICYMI: Endorsements () Suzanne Crouch is the only gubernatorial candidate who has been endorsed by more than 100 past and present elected officials in Indiana. A full list of those endorsements can be found here. () Last but not least... As we move forward, your continued support is crucial. Together, we can make history and shape a better future for all Hoosiers. Stay tuned for more updates, and let's keep the momentum going! Onward and upward, Let's keep the conversation going! Follow me on social to keep up with the campaign. Have questions, comments or concerns? Send me an email! Facebook () Twitter () Instagram () Website () mailto:info@crouchforindiana.com Email (mailto:info@crouchforindiana.com) Get Involved! Ready to make an impact? Your support plays a pivotal role in our mission to connect with Hoosiers across the state and convey our vision for Indiana's future. name: Fair Fight Action
subject: The very rights Dr. King fought for are still under attack today
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FAIR FIGHT ACTION
body:
Today, and every day, we must remember and act on Dr. King’s words. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77119015/449435534/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20240115_f_mlkday_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWU2NWM1Yy1iZWIzLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RPnuETBbbHMD5vWGCaVycMJbg3qR2na8ZC89ax2QEiE=&emci=86e65c5c-beb3-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=8ee65c5c-beb3-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=1037277)

| | Today, on Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we celebrate Dr. King’s life and legacy. He dedicated his career to fighting for civil rights and equal opportunity — to ensuring that all Americans have equal freedom to vote and equal say in the decisions that impact our lives. **But the very rights Dr. King fought for are still under attack today. ** Since the 2020 election, more than 1,300 restrictive voting bills have been introduced in state legislatures across the country. Last year, 18 states enacted 34 laws that could make voting more difficult, with the majority of these laws disproportionately affecting communities of color. **We're witnessing an attempt to erode our democracy’s highest ideals. ** Today, you will hear many politicians quote Dr. King. Some will post his words about fighting injustice as they continue to prop up false claims that the 2020 election was stolen. They will laud his call for compassion as they try to strip millions of Americans of their right to vote. And they will praise his message as they willfully make a mockery of his words for their own political gain. So, today and every day, we must not just remember Dr. King’s words, but also _act_ on them. As he said: **“The time is always ripe to do right. Now is the time to make real the promise of democracy.”** At Fair Fight Action, we will continue working to defend the freedom to vote and ensure equal access to the ballot box. Thank you for everything you do to help ensure access to democracy for all. —The Fair Fight Action Team | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77119016/449435535/1026367988?refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20240115_f_mlkday_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWU2NWM1Yy1iZWIzLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RPnuETBbbHMD5vWGCaVycMJbg3qR2na8ZC89ax2QEiE=)

name: Valerie Foushee
subject: Honoring Dr. King’s legacy.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Foushee for Congress
body:
Last year, we saw the most aggressive attempts by the Republican party to chip away at the voting rights Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. fought relentlessly for.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77119415/449437627/1540225365?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZmQzMTAzNC1iZmIzLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c5TRgJ_GoFhzKTPiBkGZP-7iII6DwNktGftwzdMi19U=&emci=0eaf7f53-81b1-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=ffd31034-bfb3-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=799895)
---
| Peter,

Today, we honor Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.’s life and legacy of service and dedication to fighting inequality and injustice. As we recommit to his long-standing mission, today is also a reminder of the work we’ve yet to accomplish.

Last year, we saw the most aggressive attempts by the Republican party to chip away at the voting rights Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. fought relentlessly for. This year, we face an uphill battle in protecting the Voting Rights Act from Republican attempts to dismantle non-discrimination clauses, impose harsh voter ID laws, and limit vote-by-mail.

Make no mistake: these Republican-led bills are further attempts to disenfranchise Black voters.

As Republicans ramp up their efforts to roll back voting rights, I don’t plan on slowing down my efforts to protect them. I’m proud to have three critical voting rights bills in committee intended to fortify our voting rights.

Today and every day, we honor Martin Luther King Jr. by fighting against inequality, protecting our right to vote, and safeguarding our democracy.

Onward,

Valerie
---

name: Mondaire Jones
subject: Celebrating the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MONDAIRE FOR CONGRESS
body:
My commitment to upholding his vision for a free, multiracial democracy has never been stronger.
Today, we celebrate the life and legacy of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

As a candidate for Congress, my commitment to fighting for his vision of a free, multiracial democracy has never been stronger.

Dr. King’s hope for our nation went far beyond equal treatment under the law. He devoted his life not just to racial justice, but economic justice, including fair wages, housing, a high-quality education, and more. He fought relentlessly for equitable access to the ballot box and the fundamental right to vote.

Nearly 56 years after his death, Dr. King’s vision of a free and fair democracy faces its greatest challenge in generations. As Republicans cling to Donald Trump’s hateful and authoritarian message, state legislatures across the country have introduced a record number of subversive election laws to disenfranchise Black and brown voters. And in Congress, Republicans oppose voting rights legislation.

**There has never been a more urgent time to fight for Dr. King’s vision and take up his call to action.**

Last year, I was honored to join Dr. King’s family in Arizona where we marched together in support of passing the John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act and the Freedom to Vote Act. As I stood alongside his family, it was clear that the best way to truly honor Dr. King’s legacy is to keep fighting in the halls of Congress for policies that will strengthen our civil rights, defend our freedoms, and get us closer to the multiracial democracy for which he gave his life.
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77119360/449437562/-460793036?refcode=em24_240115_MLK_fndd_gq_act___MJ_ettx&utm_campaign=em24_240115_MLK_fndd_gq_act___MJ_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=fad31034-bfb3-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODAzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYWQzMTAzNC1iZmIzLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lTo8MPTtIopXLy4GmkrWOQ7O_cAxXxCY9DMxolEr_rw=)
---

Dr. King never gave up. And neither will we.

Yours in the Struggle,

Mondaire

Mondaire Jones is running for Congress to finish the work he started last term. He’s ready to fight to lower costs for residents of the Hudson Valley, defend our democracy, raise wages, and protect reproductive rights for all. And he’s doing it without a penny from corporate PACs. clean_body: My commitment to upholding his vision for a free, multiracial democracy has never been stronger. Today, we celebrate the life and legacy of Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. As a candidate for Congress, my commitment to fighting for his vision of a free, multiracial democracy has never been stronger. Dr. King’s hope for our nation went far beyond equal treatment under the law. He devoted his life not just to racial justice, but economic justice, including fair wages, housing, a high-quality education, and more. He fought relentlessly for equitable access to the ballot box and the fundamental right to vote. Nearly 56 years after his death, Dr. King’s vision of a free and fair democracy faces its greatest challenge in generations. As Republicans cling to Donald Trump’s hateful and authoritarian message, state legislatures across the country have introduced a record number of subversive election laws to disenfranchise Black and brown voters. And in Congress, Republicans oppose voting rights legislation. There has never been a more urgent time to fight for Dr. King’s vision and take up his call to action. Last year, I was honored to join Dr. King’s family in Arizona where we marched together in support of passing the John R. Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act and the Freedom to Vote Act. As I stood alongside his family, it was clear that the best way to truly honor Dr. King’s legacy is to keep fighting in the halls of Congress for policies that will strengthen our civil rights, defend our freedoms, and get us closer to the multiracial democracy for which he gave his life. | = Dr. King never gave up. And neither will we. Yours in the Struggle, Mondaire Mondaire Jones is running for Congress to finish the work he started last term. He’s ready to fight to lower costs for residents of the Hudson Valley, defend our democracy, raise wages, and protect reproductive rights for all. And he’s doing it without a penny from corporate PACs. = = = PAID FOR BY MONDAIRE FOR CONGRESS P.O. BOX #873, SLEEPY HOLLOW, NY 10591

name: Dr. Kim Schrier
subject: We still need to fight for Dr. King's vision for America
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
Remembering an icon [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77119564/449438004/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYWQ0MTAzNC1iZmIzLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_97QHmn_EOgKMCwWglDwvYGRexEn7ryQOMWwykZeAH0=&emci=2e2356a4-1ab0-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&emdi=0ad41034-bfb3-ee11-bea1-0022482237da&ceid=15820338)
---
Peter,

Today, we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. - a civil rights icon who dedicated his life to the pursuit of liberty and justice for all Americans.

Working alongside activists like Bayard Rustin, my late colleague John Robert Lewis, and many others, Dr. King led the March on Washington 60 years ago. Thanks to their organizing efforts, **more than 250,000 people stood in solidarity to support his dream — of equal rights, representation, and freedoms for ALL Americans regardless of race.** This led to the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, marking a dramatic step forward in our nation's history where communities of color finally had equal representation at the ballot box.

In the decades since that historic March on Washington and the progress that came with it, we have made some incredible strides. We have also suffered some major setbacks that prove our work is far from over. MAGA Republicans are now pushing to further erode our rights and freedoms, including the right to vote in free and fair elections.

As your congresswoman, I am committed to making Dr. King's vision for America a reality. All Americans deserve justice, equality, and the right to live free from discrimination, regardless of the color of their skin, their gender, their religion, or who they love.

Today, in taking time to honor Dr. King's legacy, I hope you'll join me in doing the same.

Thank you,

Kim

| Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress

clean_body: Remembering an icon Peter, Today, we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. - a civil rights icon who dedicated his life to the pursuit of liberty and justice for all Americans. Working alongside activists like Bayard Rustin, my late colleague John Robert Lewis, and many others, Dr. King led the March on Washington 60 years ago. Thanks to their organizing efforts, more than 250,000 people stood in solidarity to support his dream — of equal rights, representation, and freedoms for ALL Americans regardless of race. This led to the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, marking a dramatic step forward in our nation's history where communities of color finally had equal representation at the ballot box. In the decades since that historic March on Washington and the progress that came with it, we have made some incredible strides. We have also suffered some major setbacks that prove our work is far from over. MAGA Republicans are now pushing to further erode our rights and freedoms, including the right to vote in free and fair elections. As your congresswoman, I am committed to making Dr. King's vision for America a reality. All Americans deserve justice, equality, and the right to live free from discrimination, regardless of the color of their skin, their gender, their religion, or who they love. Today, in taking time to honor Dr. King's legacy, I hope you'll join me in doing the same. Thank you, Kim | Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress are not tax deductible. Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress PO Box 2728 Issaquah, WA 98027 United States

name: Mandela Barnes
subject: I am kindly requesting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Long Run PAC, P
body:
#
Friends,
Today marks the halfway point to our January fundraising deadline, and we need your help to start 2024 off strong. So I am kindly requesting:
[**Will you please make a $3 contribution to support The Long Run today? Your contribution today will help keep us on track to hit our January fundraising goals.**](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=longrunpac-em-jan24.Ibl7yb&refcode=em240115-1-t2&t=1&akid=5150%2E196590%2EEsLpBg)
[CONTRIBUTE $3](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=longrunpac-em-jan24.Ibl7yb&refcode=em240115-1-t2&t=2&akid=5150%2E196590%2EEsLpBg)
The election year is in full swing and that means we’re starting to make tough budget decisions that will impact our work through November — decisions like which races we can continue to support and how many organizations we can help mobilize voters.
With big-budget decisions looming, we know that we will have to make tough choices. But if we have your support and meet our goals, our impact on critical races won’t be limited. We can support more candidates and make a real difference in tossup races that will determine whether Democrats keep the Senate and win the House.
So, we need a lot of support today from this grassroots team, and I am counting on you to contribute $3 to help us get there.
[**Can you please make a $3 contribution to The Long Run? Your support is critical to the success of our movement.**](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=longrunpac-em-jan24.Ibl7yb&refcode=em240115-1-t2&t=3&akid=5150%2E196590%2EEsLpBg)

[Express Donate: $250](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1266?c0=longrunpac-em-jan24.w31MA1&refcode=em240115-1-t2&t=26&akid=5150%2E196590%2EEsLpBg)
[Or donate another amount](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=longrunpac-em-jan24.Ibl7yb&refcode=em240115-1-t2&t=27&akid=5150%2E196590%2EEsLpBg)
My team and I are very grateful that you are a part of our movement to protect our democracy. Thank you for chipping in today, if you can afford it.
Mandela Barnes

name: Nikki Haley
subject: Why I’m running
body:
It’s hard to believe that after 11 months in this race, the first votes will take place tonight.

| |
---
| | | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2za3q5vnjfl5d4m9r7dn3ipk5duq4ocapln3jajr0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim4ob2cjh2bs)
---

Friend,
It's hard to believe that after 11 months in this race, the first votes will take place tonight.

Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, we started as an underdog but are rising in the polls and proving those who underestimate our movement wrong. Supporters like YOU have helped us put on over 150 town halls packed with fired-up supporters who are ready to get our country back on track. I've met with overflowing rooms of voters across the country, shook every hand, answered every question, and shared why I am ready to win this race and lead our country forward as your next President of the United States.

---
| [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z6ssgtpi8mfd5sudriqlp29jakf9noo5et0jabe0_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim4ob2cjh2bs)
---

**[As we enter this critical stage of our fight, I wanted to remind you why I am running for president and why your support matters.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z78m1u19pt97bck4im2rsa2dbqticlqg49pvdp0o_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim4ob2cjh2bs "As we enter this critical stage of our fight, I wanted to remind you why I am running for president and why your support matters.")**

I'm a proud daughter of legal immigrants, a wife of a combat veteran, and a mother of two. I have watched the American Dream unfold before me. My parents came to America more than 50 years ago to a country that was strong, and proud, and full of opportunities.

_**But right now, our future is slipping. **_

**[We are in a battle to save our country, and I need your help. Your continued support is so important before the first-in-the-nation Iowa Caucuses today. Can I count on you to chip in?](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zl0up3cmc39nqq1i7a9fkjl4ubeqe216pkjvtg90_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim4ob2cjh2bs "We are in a battle to save our country, and I need your help. Your continued support is so important before the first-in-the-nation Iowa Caucuses today. Can I count on you to chip in?")**

**I 'm running for President because we have a country to save!** I aim to move America upward toward freedom and strength.

Saving our country will require us to elect a candidate who not only can defeat Biden and Harris, but one who can lead our country into a brighter future.

In the polls, I defeat Biden by 17 points. I'm the strongest candidate to go toe-to-toe with Biden in November.

And when it comes to leading America into a brighter future, I know how to get it done. As governor, we cut taxes, created thousands of jobs, and revitalized our economy. Business journals started calling South Carolina the Beast of the Southeast - which I loved!

When I was Ambassador to the United Nations, I told the world that America would have the backs of our allies, and for those who did not have our backs, we were taking names! The dictators, murderers, and thieves at the UN didn't know what hit them.

The future of America is too important to hold back now.

I'm running for President of the United States. I'm fighting for a strong and proud America...And I want you by my side.

Our moment is now and our mission is clear.

**[Let's save our country and secure our future. And let's move forward, together, toward our destiny - in a strong and proud America!](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z6p62no00gt8f5ibkhsbsnkgenanlp927a0n8qbg_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim4ob2cjh2bs "Let’s save our country and secure our future. And let’s move forward, together, toward our destiny – in a strong and proud America!")**

We have a country to save. With you on my side, I know we can do it!

Thank you for everything you do.
---

My very best,

My name is Nikki Haley. I want to tell you a bit about myself and why I'm running for President of the United States.

I'm running for President because my parents came to America more than 50 years ago - an America that was strong and proud, and full of opportunities. I want them to know that America again.

I'm running for President as a military spouse. My husband is deployed and I want him and his military brothers and sisters to know their sacrifice means something - that we do love this country.

I'm running for President for the future of our kids. I have a daughter who just got married and I saw how hard it was for her and her husband to buy a home. I'm doing this for my son who's a senior in college and shouldn't have to worry about saying things on a paper that he doesn't believe in just to get an A.

I'm running for my daughter, my son, and our future generations. I want them to experience the freedom and opportunity that the American Dream has to offer.

_**I 'm running for President because we have a country to save! **_

I've seen that even on our worst days, we are blessed to live in America. But I also know that under Biden's leadership, times are tough, our future is slipping, and America can be doing so much better.

**As governor, I was dubbed a "jobs governor"** because I slashed our unemployment rate, brought jobs back to our state, and shepherded in a successful economic recovery.

**At the UN I fought for America,** made sure our allies knew we had their backs and stood up to murderers and dictators to let them know that America was taking names.

I know how to make America strong and proud because I've done it before. **And I 'll do it as your next President. **

As President, I'll revitalize our economy, support small businesses, stop Washington's spending sprees, protect our border, champion school choice, and save America's future.

**[Will you join me today and help me save our country?](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zp6dlqlmamevlr7li6i255ivsi1cm84n4p80v3a8_rp22sh2s8i6eo35cpim4ob2cjh2bs "Will you join me today and help me save our country?")**

name: Eric Sorensen
subject: We can’t be silent about things that matter
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Eric Sorensen for Illinois
body:
| | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953w5gOujJU4G2FQlGZnk4uqAqsnaOulsFtjkTh64P77vUpCYIMPI6gKOD8cNKB2HV4hm8NKZbMcG36LGa3HbWI48GfbuDq3HLMucTNIfMtq_4OA6lwz4Tmat_2V1pBiCaiHUhLTjQnvWwGdpra3PYKm1mAWBO_v70WbFoGlXbr4-FD8T7lkOfD_hCmH_R4DorZLtSvOkSe2jyX66HqMzCVbXm0LzZRwyamfyZs6MR9iPTk4LTHqCgjr-rPKQvoa1jsYM9QDg-G1vxStCgRRHmK5WE-RBqfViLgX3W8fOCkqyhVNyXowtx54NE54P0WWXmgPdT0Y8Job6DqNviiQwxiSOiLtaFERwqjgslqh8ZF_akw/430/N27fyZHwTUueyPP7b4LWUA/h0/YRdJ7DSp3Bvei5rDpzqUbaXYrXlhs0SBYiXSNWjIxqE)
---
Peter, Today is not about coming up with the right graphic that says “Happy MLK Day” and posting it for all to see. Today is a day to begin the work all over again. Every year, we need the marker to remind ourselves that there’s much to celebrate, but also so much work to do. We are incredibly lucky to live in a diverse and welcoming community. But we must also believe that those who hate can be filled with love. And the change we seek can’t just be expected, but created by all of us as one part of one community. As Martin Luther King Jr. once so profoundly said, “Our lives begin to end the day we become silent about things that matter.” We still have work to do for Dr. King’s dream to become reality. But it’s happening person by person. As your ally in Congress, I believe in using every day to help the families I serve. We have to make sure that safety, success, and happiness are no longer determined by family income, ethnicity, or the zip code where we grew up. We have to stand up against racism and push personal biases out of our lives. On this day and every day, we must stand together against hate. Today, I am reminded why we fight for what’s right. Because we know what we are fighting for. \- Eric
---

**We’re just eight hours out from the Republican presidential nominating contest kicking off with tonight’s Iowa caucuses. Here’s what you need to know →**
1. Iowa Republicans will vote for ONE GOP candidate for president tonight – and Trump could be their pick, _despite facing 91 felony counts across four criminal indictments._
2. Republicans have poured more than $100 million into ads leading up to the Iowa caucuses, using the state to propel their 2024 bids.
3. Republicans are using this caucus as a huge fundraising moment, so it’s critical we meet their momentum and don’t get left behind.

**The big takeaway:** _Every single Republican candidate running for president holds extreme views that go against our fundamental rights._ We need to prove that Democrats can build power in states like Iowa by continuing our work to elect state Dems. name: VoteVets HQ
subject: Iowa + Trump
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
Today is the day that Trump begins cementing his place as the presidential nominee for the Republican Party.
#
Derek,
Today is the day that Trump begins cementing his place as the presidential nominee for the Republican Party.
Unless there is an absolutely unprecedented upset tonight, Trump will begin accumulating delegates in Iowa.
A lot of smart people have not come around to the most likely outcome yet: That Trump and Biden will go head to head once again, with the fate of our democracy hanging in the balance.
The sooner we accept that, the better. Because we need to beat Trump.
The man who lied more than 30,000 times as president. Who refused to honor fallen Americans in a World War 1 cemetery in France. Who called Americans who made the ultimate sacrifice, Americans who were wounded in combat, “suckers” and “losers.” Who has promised to weaponize the federal government, the Department of Justice, and the Military against his political opponents.
The man who would shred our Constitution, destroy our democracy, and remake America in his loathsome image.
That guy.
So, it is time to get to work. And before the Iowa Caucus ends tonight, before Trump begins his march to the nomination, we’re asking you to chip in.
**[Make a donation of $5 to support VoteVets as we fight to preserve our democracy. Tell Trump and the GOP that we will fight them every step of the way. It will take all of us doing whatever we can to defeat him, so please. Start today.](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-jan24.1LNhS-&refcode=em240115-1-30&t=1&akid=23538%2E3013755%2ECZhlS-)**

name: Tom Barrett for Congress
subject: Offical Iowa Caucus Survey – OPEN NOW!
body:
Make your voice heard! | | | **IOWA CAUCUS SURVEY**
---
| The day is finally here, and the start of the 2024 camapign kicks off **today** with the First-In-The-Nation Iowa Caucuses. Republican voters in the Hawkeye State will come together and make their choice for who should be our nominee to take on Joe Biden. [**Will you join Iowans and make your voice heard by taking our Official Iowa Caucus Survey?**](https://mgcp02.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65a559d3f0113b1e7a5a7a29&u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.winred.com%2Ftom-barrett-for-congress%2Fiacaucussurvey&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_65a556215245307b174fbd24&l=en-US&s=yjDYiD0BofoVCYYYf9dEQKwyJKc%3D)
---
[_ _ TAKE THE SURVEY _ _](https://mgcp02.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65a559d3f0113b1e7a5a7a29&u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.winred.com%2Ftom-barrett-for-congress%2Fiacaucussurvey&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_65a556215245307b174fbd24&l=en-US&s=yjDYiD0BofoVCYYYf9dEQKwyJKc%3D)
---
This survey will take less than a minute and while you might not be trudging the snows of Iowa, you can still make your voice heard and let America know who you want to be the GOP nominee!
---
[_ _ COMPLETE HERE _ _](https://mgcp02.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65a559d3f0113b1e7a5a7a29&u=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.winred.com%2Ftom-barrett-for-congress%2Fiacaucussurvey&w=618846223a022e7ecb41d30c&c=b_65a556215245307b174fbd24&l=en-US&s=yjDYiD0BofoVCYYYf9dEQKwyJKc%3D)
---
Thanks for your input,
---
| | |

Tom Barrett, U.S. Army (Ret.)Candidate for CongressMichigan’s 7th District

name: Adam Schiff
subject: A lot on my mind
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
| Donald Trump and his allies have done everything they can to sabotage this campaign. We’re not intimidated. Let’s show them.
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=5017%2E5257348%2EsSQ2FP)
Derek, there’s a lot on my mind.
Later today, Republicans will vote in the Iowa Caucus.
While we won’t know the winner until results are called, polls are giving us a pretty good indication that Donald Trump, a man who has been impeached twice for high crimes and misdemeanors, looks to be cruising to victory. And not just in Iowa — he seems to be running away with the GOP nomination for president, despite facing four criminal indictments, two civil lawsuits, and 91 criminal counts across jurisdictions.
The Republican Party has had numerous chances to denounce Trump’s criminal behavior and turn away from this charlatan. They didn’t — landing us where we are today.
**There’s no skirting around it: Donald Trump and the toxic brand of politics he represents are the new foundation of the Republican Party.**
That gives us a choice to make: Will we let a demagogue lie, cheat, and steal his way back into power — or will we stand up forcefully, more than ever before, to fight for the values we hold dear?
**I know my answer because it’s something I’ve done my entire career.**
As the lead impeachment manager for Donald Trump’s first trial, I worked to shine a light on the former president’s misconduct.
I didn’t do it because I relished the idea of taking on the most powerful man in the world — but because our country demanded it.
Because we need leaders who are willing to take on the tough fights in service to our country and the Constitution they swore an oath to.
I’ve stood up to Trump and his allies' attacks, bullying, and partisan threats. And I’m ready to do so again in the halls of the U.S. Senate.
**Donald Trump and his allies have done everything they can to sabotage this campaign. We’re not intimidated. Let’s show them.**
**[Early voting begins in California in three weeks — where both Donald Trump and I will be on the ballot. Will you pitch in $3 to help send our fight to the U.S. Senate?](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=jan2024_abs_em.zPsl9O&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_MMG_AC90_20240115_AM1_V1_S1&email=&amount=3&t=4&akid=5017%2E5257348%2EsSQ2FP)**

name: Katie Porter
subject: Thoughts on Martin Luther King Jr. Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=36488%2E3422442%2E8KmZvv)
Derek, today we honor and remember Martin Luther King Jr., who tirelessly fought for a more just and fair society.
On Saturday, I attended the annual Los Angeles Labor Fed MLK Breakfast. The LA Labor Fed is a coalition of union and labor leaders who organize in support of progressive policies that will better the lives of workers across California. This year’s theme was “celebrating the ‘Twenty-Somethings’ that help build the Civil Rights and Labor Movements.”
It was an inspiring tribute to both young leaders who are currently leading movements for justice, and past leaders, like Martin Luther King Jr. himself, who was just 26 when he led the Montgomery Bus Boycott.
**As I say each year, we must match our words of gratitude to Dr. King with real action**. That means calling out the fact that our economy isn’t working for too many Black families, and standing up to big corporations and special interests who are desperately trying to protect the status quo. It means recognizing that we can’t tackle the climate crisis, without prioritizing communities of color where pollution has been deliberately dumped for decades.
As we build on Dr. King’s legacy, there’s much work ahead of us—thank you for being a part of the fight for a better future.
Onward,
Katie Porter

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4s-emails/?t=2&akid=36488%2E3422442%2E8KmZvv)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4s-em-update-info-footer)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4s-unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=36488%2E3422442%2E8KmZvv)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Senate
PO Box 5176
Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate

subject: Learn about the real American Founding
body:
I'd like to invite you to join me in Hillsdale College's newest free course studying the greatest Christian apologist of the 20th century, C.S. Lewis.

| | |
---
| | | | This sponsored message from Conservative Liberty Wire is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4c12464198db4db4bc94bbce75b918bf&_e=iHef46EDc8CeJ2Nhf-vr9RVfxa_AP4wyKzFfmIJEW-yy74yXH_TIv0tZ9adCQIS_Uh6O6oAM6dbDqRJ8C4hvPIdRZdhNsefyna5gvq-76KbHukwTXxM0SLFfsclhYNKwPn29tuaXFLthGTz3Xe41-Hh3LI51R9LDr6oJMH2O4ljFcPLbB6SGufYI835-VnjN9siePKScPTNUAcTbQGXzebBTfuWEadsDqRD88p9vRDP_2hDKeh5eXIvBnT-iRrn929vWnytd9zi2vnuELbVzCo1I2azBeDL6xkxFCbCgJUcDplvcQwdjumIzoqCB2CdvkxHKEEDijqTyQL0m7UEmLNJjklq1gXS19wlJXBhELPFL5FXbMqxXlxmrQvf8f9Wm5kA3OcrK2E0Wmp_J42AJmaO_2mmuF1Qx6fsXFf1X_sF2KhrxsSmiJSm3jRr63v2u) [](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4c12464198db4db4bc94bbce75b918bf&_e=iHef46EDc8CeJ2Nhf-vr9RVfxa_AP4wyKzFfmIJEW-yy74yXH_TIv0tZ9adCQIS_Uh6O6oAM6dbDqRJ8C4hvPIdRZdhNsefyna5gvq-76KbHukwTXxM0SLFfsclhYNKwPn29tuaXFLthGTz3Xe41-Hh3LI51R9LDr6oJMH2O4ljFcPLbB6SGufYI835-VnjN9siePKScPTNUAcTbQGXzebBTfuWEadsDqRD88p9vRDP_2hDKeh5eXIvBnT-iRrn929vWnytd9zi2vnuELbVzCv8XlqITi1tDSdQn0emNXEZKUWPMjbJd6h4nmllxh_kJ1QfXFXTV3sBWNCM3DTWuCeYe8TQOIoaXKCS6avIpqDU2_7qgjMA-b8OkIQZwPfQGfFDb6wrzDyd5UVTZnXxlhe-Hw6h3LraMQqNG2da_iHR8HWFIVdZYPD0n9KAtRRaL) | Hello Friend,

Thomas G. West, professor of politics at Hillsdale College, is one of the great scholars and teachers of the American Founding.

That's why we're so excited to offer a course that explains his core teachings about the American Founding, **which is now available on DVD.**

In this course, Dr. West sits down with David Azerrad, his former student and current professor at Hillsdale's Graduate School of Government, to reveal the truth about the American Founding. | **
### What students are saying
** “I appreciate the brevity of explanation and new information in a way I hadn't known or realized myself. The quizzes are helpful. Having two teachers converse brings an interesting perspective.” **- Darlena** “It helps put into context the thinking of our founders at that time. That’s very important and misunderstood by so many people today!” **- Robert**
---|---

As Dr. West explains, the Founders' political theory is so foreign to Americans today--even to scholars and teachers of American history and politics--that the task of uncovering the truth about the Founding is like unearthing something from a "deep and long-forgotten pit."

This course goes about this task by examining the actual words and actions of the Founders, and it brings their theory to life by comparing it to the dominant views of contemporary America.

The result is an exciting and often surprising account of how the Founders established a free government in America and what they thought was necessary to sustain it.

As you know, our online courses are free. That's why we depend on the generosity of people like you to help us share the course with as many people as possible.

Would you consider making a tax-deductible donation to help promote this course?

And now, for any donation of $100 or more, you will receive this beautiful DVD box set for **" The Real American Founding: A Conversation,"** by clicking the link below.

[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/real-american-founding-dvd](https://links.conservativelibertywire.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=4c12464198db4db4bc94bbce75b918bf&_e=iHef46EDc8CeJ2Nhf-vr9RVfxa_AP4wyKzFfmIJEW-yy74yXH_TIv0tZ9adCQIS_Uh6O6oAM6dbDqRJ8C4hvPIdRZdhNsefyna5gvq-76KbHukwTXxM0SLFfsclhYNKwPn29tuaXFLthGTz3Xe41-Hh3LI51R9LDr6oJMH2O4ljFcPLbB6SGufYI835-VnjN9siePKScPTNUAcTbQGXzebBTfuWEadsDqRD88p9vRDP_2hDKeh5eXIvBnT-iRrn929vWnytd9zi2vnuELbVzCsppTcMTsINxkadxDgK-SEPpGJSWm9rpO22BamGj1X2s_YHaoKgQ2lwPBtwZyikfzk0Ew8REeXy71jbuVRMTXQccrAZqRmcNdFbSYjHCZNat3iu5xIXx0sdGw7vTt8y4_lNbbtgJiUvuBdyE34-5HGNuREYczut_4-bR5ZBV4rma)

I hope you'll join us in this fun and important work of sharing the truth about the American Founding with your fellow Americans.

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, '09
Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

name: John Padora
subject: Honoring his legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Padora for Colorado
body:
Today, we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Dr. King fought tirelessly as a leader of the Civil Rights Movement. His contributions to our country are immeasurable, and today we focus on our commitment to living through his legacy of kindness, equality, and service.
__ | | | [](http://links.padoraforcongress.com/u/click?_t=1632d2fd715245289db23a5b99e588cb&_m=b0553f157c154fd2abf45f4498fd1a91&_e=Suz5kqIeSSrmpHzxAmY5bLFmcaXh8kplbm4ejLfhmmBU-7QIldgXaXPZOKJJ6bPC7KhnVEJbek3eCLZiT90lgsJgalHdXKG22TdjPQF3CJAaFhESWORsTUbygQR-9GL0s5Zv302z3Qc3w_YJyEO0RUxRnX7k7C508wbdoFNrRUWW6j99XrAFi8VQBK81SYg6R_tHG6U-DD5UWvsI-YhJIA%3D%3D) Friends - Today, we honor the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Dr. King fought tirelessly as a leader of the Civil Rights Movement. His contributions to our country are immeasurable, and today we focus on our commitment to living through his legacy of kindness, equality, and service. [](http://links.padoraforcongress.com/u/click?_t=1632d2fd715245289db23a5b99e588cb&_m=b0553f157c154fd2abf45f4498fd1a91&_e=Suz5kqIeSSrmpHzxAmY5bLFmcaXh8kplbm4ejLfhmmBU-7QIldgXaXPZOKJJ6bPCqehkWvR3uYTHN51pqg2UhpYKxvovlmm1HNW2zwsjkbTRu73OGyL94MaPQP_VhRrdtHTsbgzOf3ZFe7uY4XRXCUdSzfpfoXHkaLJ3M4McO1teNGG1c6YqQCzd0FYEzyz6Q-E3KzZs5C4rEvrCAPAQrg%3D%3D) One of my favorite quotes from Dr. King that I carry with me as a husband, a father, and most recently a Congressional candidate is, “The time is always right to do the right thing.” In times like these, when division seems to be more prominent than unity, it’s important to remember it always comes back to the value of standing up for what's right and giving a voice to those who need it. I encourage you to do some good for your friends, family, or community today and remember to live through the lessons that Dr. King’s life and legacy have taught us. Happy Martin Luther King Jr. Day! name: Ritchie Torres
subject: I will never forget where I came from, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458
body:
When you invest in this campaign, you’re investing in community.

| Hi, it’s Ritchie. It’s been a while since I introduced myself to you, Peter.

[If you’ve been on Team Ritchie from day 1, you can click here to skip my story and support our grassroots movement »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/77121727/449445880/-866719404?amount=3&refcode=RTFC_20240115_EMDF_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjE2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZjk1MmU2NC1jM2IzLWVlMTEtYmVhMS0wMDIyNDgyMjM3ZGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=s61F5Aj94wyBLsQSfBBYJoYC8ktNhU7YT2K_xYoZPno=)

**_Here’s a little bit about me:_**

📍 I was born and raised in the Bronx.

🏠 Growing up with a single mom, public housing was a lifeline for my family — so I’ve dedicated my career to fighting for affordable housing and building social safety nets to eliminate poverty.

🏳️🌈 I’m the first openly LGBTQ+ Afro-Latino man in Congress. My lived experience as a gay Black man motivates me to fight _every day_ until we are fully equal in the eyes of the law.

👊🏽 I’m a tireless advocate for Social Security and Medicare. I believe proper health care cannot be limited to physical health, and I’ll continue to advocate tirelessly for access to mental health care.

**_And here’s why my story matters for you:_**

When you invest in this campaign, you’re investing in _community._

From fighting for affordable housing to community initiatives to Medicare and more, I know this grassroots movement is capable of _changing the game_ for the Bronx.

**I’ve got your back, Peter, and I’m hoping you’ve got mine:[Will you chip in $3, or whatever you can afford, to help me continue fighting for you? Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in.
